fields.description: A C10 monoterpenoid alcohol produced via engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through metabolic engineering of the mevalonate pathway, fusion protein strategies, and fermentation optimization.
Ophiobolin skeleton compound with 5-8-5 tricyclic structure, precursor to bioactive ophiobolins
Novel ophiobolin intermediate with potent cytotoxicity, oxidation product of ophiobolin F
Known bioactive ophiobolin compound produced through P450 oxidation
Health-benefiting substances with prebiotic effect produced by depolymerizing agarose using endo-type β-agarase

fields.grna_map: CRISPR-Cas9 system was used for genomic integration of BpGH16A gene
fields.description: A specific neoagarooligosaccharide containing various bioactive properties, produced by engineered S. boulardii from agarose

A volatile hydrocarbon produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae for industrial applications such as rubber manufacturing, perfumes, and pesticides. Achieved via dual regulation of cytoplasmic and mitochondrial acetyl-CoA utilization.
A terpenoid found in citrus peel waste, which acts as a yeast fermentation inhibitor. Its concentration was reduced from 0.21% to 0.01% after popping pretreatment.
Monoterpenoid produced by engineered Yarrowia lipolytica.
Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain producing α-farnesene via metabolic engineering, achieving a titer of 10.4 g/L in fed-batch fermentation.
Sesquiterpenoid produced by engineered Yarrowia lipolytica.
Sesquiterpenoid produced by engineered Yarrowia lipolytica.

A high-value sesquiterpene hydrocarbon produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae, serving as a precursor for commercially relevant sesquiterpenes. Generated via metabolic engineering of the mevalonate pathway and FPP branch point.
Sesquiterpenoid produced by engineered Yarrowia lipolytica.
Sesquiterpenoid produced by engineered Yarrowia lipolytica.
Sesquiterpenoid produced by engineered Yarrowia lipolytica.
Sesquiterpenoid produced by engineered Yarrowia lipolytica.
Diterpenoid (Vitamin A) produced by engineered Yarrowia lipolytica.
Accumulated in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae YUG37-ERG1 by repressing ERG1 expression with doxycycline, yielding up to 7.89 mg/g dry biomass and 18.0 mg/L from grass juice.
Triterpene derivative produced from lupeol oxidation by P450 enzymes in yeast, mentioned as an example of triterpene functionalization.

An active triterpene compound and precursor of high-value ginsenosides, exhibiting promising anticancer activity. Produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through cofermentation of glucose and xylose.

A triterpenoid side product produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing the BvBAS (Beta vulgaris β-amyrin synthase) enzyme, which exhibits multifunctional oxidosqualene cyclase activity.
Triterpenoid produced by engineered Yarrowia lipolytica.

A carotenoid pigment produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through continuous self-cycling fermentation that recycles wastewater and biomass residue. Achieved 5.88 g/L in 70 L fermenters with 22.25% higher titer than traditional methods, reducing production costs by 29.6% and chemical oxygen demand by 64.0%. Possesses antioxidant, anti-cancer, and cardiovascular disease prevention properties for use in functional foods and as a food colorant.

A carotenoid pigment that serves as the precursor for β-ionone production. Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains accumulated up to 32 mg/gDCW of total carotenoids (primarily β-carotene) through heterologous expression of carotenogenic genes (CrtE, CrtYB, CrtI) from Xanthophyllomyces dendrorhous, representing the highest concentration reported for a constitutive expression system in yeast.
An intermediate ketocarotenoid produced when H. pluvialis β-carotene ketolase (BKT) is expressed in S. cerevisiae.

A lipophilic carotenoid precursor that accumulates in yeast cellular membranes and serves as the direct substrate for CCD2 cleavage in the engineered crocetin biosynthetic pathway. Its biosynthesis is temperature-dependent and synchronized with crocetin production using the GAL4M9 switch system.
A carotenoid-derivative pigment with high antioxidant activity, produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains through metabolic engineering of the astaxanthin biosynthesis pathway.

A commercially attractive industrial fragrance with woody-violet aroma, produced as an apocarotenoid by enzymatic cleavage of β-carotene using engineered carotenoid cleavage dioxygenase 1 (CCD1) from Petunia hybrida in Saccharomyces cerevisiae. The highest producing strain achieved 33 mg/L in shake flask cultures, representing the highest titer reported to date in yeast.
Terpenoid derivative (plant hormone) produced by engineered Yarrowia lipolytica.
Terpenoid derivative (plant hormone) produced by engineered Yarrowia lipolytica.
Terpenoid derivative (plant hormone) produced by engineered Yarrowia lipolytica.
Red-colored molecule produced by the type III polyketide synthase RppA through one-step conversion of malonyl-CoA; used as a colorimetric reporter for malonyl-CoA levels.
A versatile monomer produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae, used as a petrochemical replacement for plastics, rubbers, and as polyitaconic acid.

Produced via malonyl-CoA reductase-dependent pathway in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Used as a precursor for acrylic acid and acrylic esters, with applications in polymers, adhesives, coatings, and biodegradable polyesters. Achieved 9.8 g/L titer at pH 5 in carbon-limited fed-batch cultivation.
Produced in E. coli with a HcaR-based biosensor for real-time detection and yield improvement.
A high-value chemical precursor used for nylon, coatings, plastics, and plasticizers production. Can be produced via chemical hydrogenation of biotechnologically synthesized cis,cis-muconic acid.
Biosynthesized in E. coli using an AraC-based biosensor for screening rate-limiting enzymes.
Aromatic amino acid whose production was improved through FREP evolution in E. coli, resulting in 5-fold increase.
Detected in cell-free systems using a PsiR-based biosensor for enzymatic production monitoring.
Produced in E. coli using a native riboswitch-based biosensor (5'-UTR of LysC) for strain enrichment.
Biosynthesized in E. coli with an artificial riboswitch-based biosensor for producer screening.
Detected in E. coli with an artificial riboswitch-based biosensor for specificity to lactam compounds.

Engineered production of theophylline (1,3-dimethylxanthine) as an alternative methylxanthine product in yeast strains, achieving titers of 61.4 μg/L in bioreactor experiments. Known as an antiasthma therapeutic compound.
Detected in S. cerevisiae with an artificial riboswitch-based biosensor for product monitoring.
Produced in E. coli and screened using a riboswitch-based biosensor (5'-UTR of BtuB) for high yield.
Monitored in cell-free/liposome systems using an artificial riboswitch-based biosensor.
A stilbenoid antioxidant commercially produced by engineered yeast strains, acquired by Evolva AG.
Detected using a GPCR-based biosensor for extracellular sensing in metabolic engineering.

A flavonol produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain QUE44 expressing 8 plant genes (PAL, C4H, 4CL, CHS, CHI, F3H, F30H, FLS). Production reached 0.26 mg/L from p-coumaric acid and 0.38 mg/L from naringenin precursor; trace amounts from phenylalanine in defined media.

Produced from industrial waste glycerol via anaerobic fermentation by Saccharomyces cerevisiae (instant baker's yeast) at 25°C, achieving 42.3 wt% yield with 93.6 wt% glycerol conversion. Used as a high-value specialty chemical in polyester fibers, films, coatings, textile industry, food packaging, lubricants, and medicine. Non-flammable, low toxicity, biodegradable, and miscible with water, alcohols, and ethers.
Produced from glucose in a complex cell-free system involving a purge valve for regulating reducing equivalents, achieving titers up to 16 g/l, an order of magnitude higher than in cells.
Monoterpene produced by engineered yeast using orthogonal NPP pathway; used as a flavor and potential biofuel.

Produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via XYL1-encoded xylose reductase converting xylose. Strain SCX-5 achieves theoretical maximum yield of 1.00 g/g at 0.162 g/L/h, with 85.7% higher titer than glucose/xylose mixtures. Used as sugar substitute and platform chemical.
Produced from starch using a five-enzyme cell-free system at the 20,000-l scale, demonstrating scalability.
Produced via in vitro metabolic engineering using a cell-free multi-enzyme system.

Produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae at 460.2 mg/L through B12-dependent conversion of meso-2,3-butanediol via butanone. Requires anaerobic cultivation of GPD1/GPD2 deletion strain to provide NADH surplus for the secondary alcohol dehydrogenase step.

fields.grna_map: Used for ALD6 deletion in strain VSY19
fields.description: Gasoline substitute produced via engineered acetoacetyl-CoA-dependent pathway in Saccharomyces cerevisiae, achieving titers up to 859 mg/L and yields of 0.071 g/g glucose through optimization of acetyl-CoA synthesis, NADH levels, and trans-2-enoyl-CoA reductase expression.
Produced from starch or sugars in a cell-free system using a synthetic enzymatic pathway, achieving nearly quantitative conversion with a theoretical yield of 12 H2 per glucose molecule.
Red-violet natural food dye produced in Saccharomyces cerevisiae from glucose via heterologous expression of CYP76AD1W13L, MjDOD, and glucosyltransferases. Achieved titer of 17 mg/L.
Betaxanthin pigment abundant in Beta vulgaris, with glutamine as the substituent. Used as a natural yellow pigment.

A violacein derivative produced as a mixture with violacein in engineered Escherichia coli hosts expressing the vioABCDE gene cluster. Can be produced as a single end-product (1.92 g/L) by expressing VioABCE without VioD. Shows weaker antiparasitic activity compared to violacein.
fields.source_filename: Microbial synthesis of violacein pigment and its potential applications.pdf

fields.description: Poly-(3-hydroxybutyrate-co-3-hydroxyvalerate) biodegradable polymer scaffolds incorporated with violacein for regenerative bone tissue engineering. Implanted in Wistar rats, they show no inflammatory reaction and promote osteointegration.
A natural antibiotic flavin of reddish-orange color, produced by engineered Komagataella phaffii yeast. Effective against Gram-positive bacteria and some parasitic protozoa.
fields.source_filename: ROS-Efficient production of bacterial antibiotics aminoriboflavin and roseoflavin in eukaryotic microorganisms, yeasts.pdf

fields.description: Vitamin B2, overproduced by engineered yeast strains as a precursor for flavin antibiotic synthesis. Accumulated at high titers during fermentation.
fields.source_filename: ROS-Efficient production of bacterial antibiotics aminoriboflavin and roseoflavin in eukaryotic microorganisms, yeasts.pdf

fields.description: Produced by phosphorylation of riboflavin by flavokinase (PfFK) in Plasmodium falciparum; essential cofactor for enzymes like dihydroorotate dehydrogenase.
fields.source_filename: ROS-Mutation of the P. falciparum flavokinase confers resistance 2 to roseoflavin and 8-aminoriboflavin.pdf

fields.description: Phosphorylated analogue of roseoflavin produced by PfFK; acts as a toxic FMN analogue that may inhibit flavoenzymes in P. falciparum.
fields.source_filename: ROS-Mutation of the P. falciparum flavokinase confers resistance 2 to roseoflavin and 8-aminoriboflavin.pdf

fields.description: Phosphorylated analogue of 8-aminoriboflavin generated by PfFK; functions as a toxic FMN analogue inhibiting parasite growth.
fields.source_filename: ROS-Mutation of the P. falciparum flavokinase confers resistance 2 to roseoflavin and 8-aminoriboflavin.pdf

fields.description: Pentacyclic triterpenoid compound with anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidation, anti-diabetic, anti-bacterial, anti-metastatic, anti-angiogenic, and anti-tumorigenic activities. Produced via metabolic engineering in Saccharomyces cerevisiae using amyrin C-28 oxidase and cytochrome P450 reductase.
Used in high-end fragrance manufacture; produced by specific diTPS combinations in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

Exhibits antibiotic activities and serves as a starting material for semisynthesis of pharmaceuticals; stereoselective production of (+)-manool achieved at 310 mg/L in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
Clerodane-type diterpene, skeleton of salvinorin A (lead drug candidate for mental disorders); produced via novel diTPS combination.

An abietane-type diterpene olefin produced in engineered Escherichia coli and Saccharomyces cerevisiae. It serves as a key intermediate in tanshinone biosynthesis, with previous work achieving production levels of 365 mg/L in yeast.
Synthetic ambergris substitute used in high-end fragrances; produced via stereoselective biosynthesis in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

C6-C8 alcohols like 3-methyl-1-pentanol, 1-pentanol, 1-hexanol, 4-methyl-1-pentanol, 4-methyl-1-hexanol, and 5-methyl-1-heptanol produced by extending ketoacid carbon chains via engineered '+1' pathway.
Isoprenoid compound produced by engineered S. cerevisiae PE-2 strain from sugarcane juice by Amyris in Brazil, used as a precursor for biofuels and chemicals.
Potential aviation fuel component produced in E. coli by pinene synthase and geranyl diphosphate synthase, yielding 1.5 mg/L.
Produced by encapsulated S. cerevisiae in equivalent yields to free cell cultures, demonstrating that the NaCS-PDMDAAC encapsulation system preserves normal cellular metabolic function.
An intermediate in the melatonin biosynthesis pathway produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae from glucose via acetylation of serotonin.
Downstream product derived from muconic acid, used in PET production.

A polyphenolic compound with health-benefit effects, including anticarcinogenic activity, produced from ferulic acid by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing heterologous enzymes such as 4-coumarate-CoA ligase (4CL1) from Arabidopsis thaliana or feruloyl-CoA synthetase (FerA) from Pseudomonas paucimobilis, and type III polyketide synthases (PKSs) from Oryza sativa or Curcuma longa.

A higher alcohol flavor compound produced by diploid S. cerevisiae strains. Production was monitored to ensure genetic modifications for TTMP production did not alter other important flavor substances. The AY-D1B2 strain showed a 23.71% decrease.

Microbial cell mass produced during fermentation. Biomass yields on consumed sugar were relatively constant, but biomass concentration increased from 0.64 to 1.78 g/L with increasing glucose concentration in the feed. The engineered strain showed stable enzyme activities for over 40 generations in continuous culture.
Accumulates at high levels (up to 1 g/liter) early in fermentation in the engineered strain, impairing growth and fermentation performance

Detected as an additional product in bioreactor cultivations of engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain UBR2CBS-DHA-SA-AnDCT-02. Accumulated up to 2 g/L temporarily during the process before decreasing.

fields.grna_map: CRISPR-Cas9 targeting YGLCτ3 locus (SA module integration) and YPRCτ3 locus (AnDCT-02 transporter integration)
fields.description: Platform chemical produced from glycerol and CO2 using engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain UBR2CBS-DHA-SA-AnDCT-02. The strain contains the DHA pathway module, SA module (MDH3-R, RofumR, TbFRDg-R), and heterologous dicarboxylic acid transporter AnDCT-02. Maximum titer of 10.7 g/L with yield of 0.22 g/g glycerol achieved in shake flask batch cultures.
Oxidation product of furfural by encapsulated Saccharomyces cerevisiae, formed in small amounts.

A sesquiterpene produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing cubebol synthase from Citrus paradisi. Production was enhanced through metabolic engineering including deletion of GDH1 (NADPH-dependent glutamate dehydrogenase), overexpression of GDH2 (NADH-dependent glutamate dehydrogenase), and overexpression of the catalytic domain of HMG-CoA reductase (tHMG1). Maximum titer of 8.4 mg/L was achieved in batch fermentation.

fields.grna_map: gRNA used for gene deletions (e.g., ROX1, YJL064W, YPL062W) and genomic integrations
fields.description: A tricyclic sesquiterpene produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae, widely used in perfumes and cosmetics.
C10 fatty acid ethyl ester flavor compound with floral odor, produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae at 13.87 mg/L (9.05-fold increase over wild-type).
Long chain alkyl ester synthesized in miniemulsion system with altered cutinase selectivity
Ester produced in miniemulsion showing high esterification rates with cutinase selectivity shift
C6 fatty acid ethyl ester flavor compound with anise seed aroma, produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae at 7.53 mg/L (25.89-fold increase over wild-type).
Linear alkyl ester achieving 88% yield with Amano lipase PS catalysis
Ester showing maximum reaction rate of 0.72 mmol mg⁻¹min⁻¹ with Amano lipase PS

Chiral alcohol produced via enantioselective reduction of o-chloroacetophenone using engineered whole-cell catalysts (E. coli and S. cerevisiae) co-expressing Candida tenuis xylose reductase and NADH recycling systems.

By-product in engineered DHAA production pathway. Also an artemisinin precursor but requires additional chemical hydrogenation. Metabolic engineering aimed to minimize AA while maximizing DHAA production.

Anti-inflammatory drug and intermediate for steroid synthesis, produced via one-step biotransformation of acetylated cortexolone using engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing Absidia orchidis steroid 11β-hydroxylation system (CYP5311B2, CPR, Cyt b5) and Cochliobolus lunatus transporter ClCDR4.
A non-ribosomal peptide antibiotic precursor produced in Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
Benzylisoquinoline alkaloids produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
A neutral lipid composed of three fatty acids attached to a glycerol molecule, used for energy storage and industrial applications like biofuels, surfactants, and lubricants.
A higher aromatic alcohol with a rose-like fragrance, widely used in perfume, cosmetic, and pharmaceutical industries. Produced via the Ehrlich pathway in Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

Total carotenoid production reached 11 mg/g DCW (72.57 mg/L) in engineered yeast strain YXWP-53 through pathway optimization and carbon source control. Production increased 367-fold compared to initial strain.
Fatty-acid-derived compounds mentioned as examples of renewable biofuels produced by engineering fatty acid biosynthesis.
Production from glucose and xylose using engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains IBB14LA1 and IBB14LA1_5. Yields and productivities are influenced by aeration conditions and carbon source.
A fragrance sesquiterpene produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing a santalene synthase from Clausena lansium.

Produced de novo in Saccharomyces cerevisiae from fatty aldehyde intermediates via endogenous aldehyde reductases (ADHs). Attained a titer of ~20 mg/L in shake flask cultures. Used in cosmetics, skin care, detergents, surfactants, solvents, and lubricants.
A cyclic compound produced via biotransformation of benzaldehyde substrate by immobilized Saccharomyces cerevisiae yeast cells in semicontinuous fermentation processes.

Early precursor for high-value aromatic compounds and starter compound for the commercial influenza antiviral Tamiflu®. Produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains through overexpression of mutant ARO1 enzyme with disrupted shikimate kinase activity.
Complex plant secondary metabolites demonstrating the great potential of using yeast as a host to produce specialized pharmaceuticals.
Produced by heterologous expression of FAR enzymes (AmFAR1 or Maqu_2220) in engineered S. cerevisiae, achieving yields up to 7.84 mg/g CDW with chain lengths up to C22.
Lupane-type triterpenoid precursor to betulinic acid, accumulated as main product in engineered S. cerevisiae fermentations.
Intermediate in the betulinic acid pathway, produced by engineered S. cerevisiae.
An antitumor triterpenoid from Ganoderma lucidum, produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via metabolic engineering and optimization of CYP5150L8 and iGLCPR expression.
Branched-chain higher alcohol (fusel alcohol) produced by S. cerevisiae during high-sugar fermentation, affecting wine sensory properties at concentrations of 29.3–43.0 mg/L

Plant-derived triterpenoid with pharmacological properties such as cytotoxicity, anti-HIV, anti-HSV, anti-inflammatory, and antidiabetic effects. Produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae by co-expressing BfOSC2 (germanicol synthase) from Bauhinia forficata and CYP716A49 (triterpene C-28 oxidase) from Beta vulgaris.

Tyrosol is a phenethyl alcohol derivative with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, used as a pharmaceutical intermediate and precursor for drugs against cardiovascular disease, cancer, and viruses. Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain GFT-4 produced 126.74 ± 6.70 mg/g DCW at 48 h, representing a 440-fold improvement over the control strain.

fields.grna_map: CRISPR/Cas9 system used for gene deletions and integrations
fields.description: Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing UDP-glycosyltransferase produced 1575.45 ± 19.35 mg/L in shake flasks and 26.55 ± 0.43 g/L in 5L bioreactors via glycosylation of tyrosol.
Nitrate ester explosive derived from 1,2,4-butanetriol, used in double-base propellants with low-temperature explosion, shock stability, and safety features.
Intermediate in BT biosynthetic pathway from xylose, accumulated as major byproduct during fermentation in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
Desired product of taxadien-5α-hydroxylase (CYP725A4) oxidation, second intermediate in paclitaxel pathway.
Third paclitaxel intermediate, produced by acetylation of T5αol catalyzed by taxadien-5α-ol O-acetyltransferase (TAT).
Side-product of CYP725A4 oxidation, a monooxygenated taxane by-product.
Side-product of CYP725A4 oxidation, a monooxygenated taxane by-product.
Diterpenoid alcohol formed via degradation of geranylgeranyl diphosphate (GGPP) by endogenous phosphatases in engineered strains.
Dioxygenated taxane side-product from CYP725A4 activity.
Polyphenolic stilbene compound extracted from grapes and metabolized during fermentation, showing strain-dependent concentrations of 0.48–1.42 mg/L

A sterol produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains as part of the mevalonate pathway. The metabolic engineering strategy significantly increased ergosterol content, with the triple over-expression strain reaching 69.6 mg/g dry weight. Ergosterol is the dominant terpenoid in yeast and a key structural component of fungal cell membranes, serving as an indicator of enhanced carbon flux through the sterol biosynthetic pathway.
Produced using Saccharomyces cerevisiae from lignocellulosic hydrolysates of Norway spruce and sugarcane bagasse. Treatment with sodium borohydride improved ethanol yield and productivity.

Nanocrystals produced by extending enzymatic hydrolysis to 144 hours with temperature reduction from 50°C to 35°C, showing crystallinity index of 83%, length of 260 nm, diameter of 15 nm, and aspect ratio of 15.

Ester compound produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae overexpressing various Eat1 homologs through in vivo alcoholysis activity. The process combines supplemented butyl butyrate with endogenously produced ethanol to generate ethyl butyrate, with Wan-Eat1 showing the highest production of 0.21 mM.
Volatile compound (ethyl ester) produced during fermentation. Levels not influenced by quinoxyfen addition.
Ester with banana flavor. Significantly higher in mixed fermentations, particularly with WB-06 and Belgian Wheat strains.

Fruity and floral aromatic ester produced by Saccharomyces cerevisiae and Pichia kluyveri during green coffee bean fermentation. Enhances fruity attributes in roasted coffee, with higher production in P. kluyveri-fermented beans.
Fusel alcohol (higher alcohol) produced during fermentation. Biosynthesis seemed activated at moderate quinoxyfen amounts.
Key organic acid contributing to liquor flavor. Present in fermentation products from both solid-state and submerged processes.

Short-chain fatty acid (C8-FA) produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via mutations in fatty acid synthase (FAS) domains. Achieved extracellular concentration of 245.0 mg/L under optimized conditions.
High-optical-purity D-lactic acid produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through systematic optimization of D-lactate dehydrogenase (D-LDH) expression.
Ethanol produced from holocellulosic materials, including terrestrial and aquatic biomass (e.g., algae, seaweed like Kappaphycus alvarezii). Aims to increase clean energy supply and recycle residues.
High-protein yeast biomass produced as byproduct during ethanol fermentation, serving as high-quality nutrient additive for feed purposes.
The (+)-enantiomer of limonene produced by expressing a (+)-limonene synthase from Citrus limon in Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Recovered via headspace trapping and dodecane overlay methods.
The (-)-enantiomer of limonene produced by expressing a (-)-limonene synthase from Perilla frutescens in Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Recovered via headspace trapping and dodecane overlay methods.

A higher alcohol flavor compound produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains with deletions in amino acid metabolism genes. Production levels vary depending on which gene is deleted, affecting Baijiu (Chinese liquor) fermentation characteristics.

Produced by the engineered PB2 hybrid yeast in batch culture under aerobic conditions, reaching a concentration of 70 g/l. It is a polyhydroxy alcohol that accumulates as a compatible solute in response to high osmotic pressure.
An antioxidant, immunity enhancer, and detoxifier used in medicine, food additives, and cosmetics. Produced via high-cell-density fed-batch fermentation using engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

A five-carbon sugar (pentose) produced from D-glucose via the pentose phosphate pathway intermediates in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains. NMR analysis showed about 60% of the produced D-ribose was derived from D-glucose.

A sugar alcohol (pentitol) produced from D-glucose via the pentose phosphate pathway in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains. Co-produced with D-ribose, with yields up to 1 g l−1 in specific strains.
An acyclic sesquiterpene alcohol accumulated in yeast cells deficient in squalene synthase activity. Also produced by recombinant S. cerevisiae overexpressing mevalonate pathway genes.
An intermediate in sterol biosynthesis that accumulates intracellularly due to overexpression of HMG1. Produced alongside GGOH in the engineered diploid yeast strain.

A bio-based bulk chemical produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain YPH499/dPdAdG/BDN6-10/FLO1 in a continuous airlift bioreactor. The strain consumed over 90 g/L of glucose and stably produced more than 30 g/L of 2,3-BDO over 380 hours with a maximum productivity of 7.64 g/L/h.
Accumulated as a byproduct due to transglycosylation activity of β-glucosidase during cellobiose fermentation.

Produced from L-arabinose by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing L-arabinose/D-galactose 1-dehydrogenase (Rl AraDH) from Rhizobium leguminosarum bv. trifolii and galactose permease Gal2. Yield: 18 g/L at a rate of 248 mg/L/h with 86% conversion efficiency.
Produced as a byproduct by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing Rl AraDH when grown on D-galactose. Yield: 1.0 g/L.
Mentioned as a downstream product for which L-arabonate serves as a precursor. Used as an intermediate for manufacturing propellants and cholesterol-lowering drugs.
Microbially produced lipids from engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae, used for biofuels and other industrial applications.
Major higher alcohol (isoamyl alcohol) produced during fermentation, with significant strain-dependent variation in concentration (163.7–232.7 mg/L) in high-alcohol wines

A potential prebiotic fructooligosaccharide (FOS) with β-(2→6) linkages, produced by engineered yeast cultures expressing the Ffase-Leu196 variant. Represents ~70% of total FOS synthesized, reaching ~116 g/L after 4 hours.
Short-chains of fructosyl units with prebiotic properties, specifically 6-kestose representing more than 95% of total FOS produced by the engineered yeast system.

A vitamin-like molecule with GRAS status, essential for shuttling activated acyl residues across intracellular membranes in eukaryotes. Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains were able to synthesize L-carnitine from trimethyllysine (TML) by reconstructing the four-step carnitine biosynthesis pathway from Neurospora crassa.
An intermediate ketocarotenoid produced when H. pluvialis β-carotene ketolase (BKT) is expressed in S. cerevisiae.

D-xylonate is a platform chemical produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae (yeast) expressing D-xylose dehydrogenase. It can be used as a complexing agent, chelator, concrete dispersant, and precursor for co-polyamides, hydrogels, and 1,2,4-butanetriol. It has potential as a substitute for D-gluconate in pharmaceuticals, food products, solvents, adhesives, dyes, paints, and polishes. Production yields up to 50 g/L from D-xylose.
Alcoholic beverage produced by fermenting durian pulp with Saccharomyces cerevisiae yeast, with ethanol and aroma compounds generated during fermentation.
Higher alcohol produced during fermentation, with higher levels at pH 3.1 (up to 64.52 mg/L) imparting ethereal flavor notes to wine.

A higher alcohol flavor compound produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains with deletions in amino acid metabolism genes. Production increases with CAN1 or GAT1 deletion and decreases with BAT2 deletion, influencing Baijiu taste and aroma.
Significant ester odorant with high odor activity value (>100) in Chinese light-fragrant liquor. Production varies by yeast genotype and fermentation method.
Purified from sweet sorghum juice using ultrafiltration and nanofiltration (NTR-7450 membrane) to obtain a sucrose-rich fraction (143.2 g L−1 sucrose) for sugar refining.

Produced via engineered glyoxylate cycle in Saccharomyces cerevisiae and Kluyveromyces lactis using glyoxylate reductase (GLYR1) expression and deletion of malate synthase/isocitrate dehydrogenase genes. Highest titer: 15 g/L in K. lactis bioreactor cultivation.
Intermediate in the Dahms pathway produced from D-xylonic acid by heterologous D-xylonate dehydratase XylD from Caulobacter crescentus in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
By-product formed by reduction of 2-keto-3-deoxyxylonic acid by endogenous yeast reductases in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing the oxidative D-xylose pathway.
By-product formed from 2-keto-3-deoxyxylonic acid via carboxylation and oxidation in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing the oxidative D-xylose pathway.

Lipids produced by oleaginous yeasts (Lipomyces starkeyi and Rhodotorula babjevae) from the enzymatic hydrolysate of wheat straw residue. R. babjevae produced lipids with a yield of 0.17 g per g consumed glucose and a rate of 0.18 g/L h.
A fatty acid produced in considerable amounts by Rhodotorula babjevae when grown on the hydrolysate, increasing from 1.5% to 21.8% of total fatty acids.
A polyunsaturated fatty acid produced by both Lipomyces starkeyi and Rhodotorula babjevae when grown on the hydrolysate. R. babjevae produced it up to 5.0% of total fatty acids.
A polyunsaturated fatty acid produced by Rhodotorula babjevae when grown on the hydrolysate, reaching a proportion of 13.3% of total fatty acids.
Direct precursor of pentacyclic triterpenes accumulated in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae by repressing ERG7 with a copper-regulated promoter (PCTR3) to redirect flux from sterol synthesis.
Non-native isoprenoid compound produced via engineered mevalonate pathway in Saccharomyces cerevisiae, serving as a precursor to the antimalarial drug artemisinin.
Anti-malarial drug precursor whose production in yeast was enhanced by suppressing squalene synthase (ERG9) using regulated promoters.
Major trisaccharide product formed during fructo-oligosaccharide synthesis from sucrose using invertase from Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
A neofructooligosaccharide (neoFOS) with β-(2→6) linkage to glucose, produced by yeast cultures. Suggested to have potential antineoplastic effects.
A sucrose isomer disaccharide produced by direct fructosylation of glucose by Ffase. Shows potential prebiotic properties by stimulating Lactobacillus growth.

De novo biosynthesis from glucose in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via heterologous expression of tyrosine ammonia-lyase (TAL), p-coumaric acid 3-hydroxylase (C3H), and cytochrome P450 reductase (CPR1). Achieved titers up to 11.432 mg L⁻¹ in optimized YPD medium.

Prebiotic oligosaccharides produced via transfructosylation of sucrose using Aureobasidium pullulans in co-culture with Saccharomyces cerevisiae. The strategy achieves 119 g L-1 concentration with 67% purity by simultaneous consumption of small saccharides.

An emerging bulk chemical used as a monomer for producing biodegradable and biocompatible plastics, and as the base for the new polyester PTT. Produced from glycerol or glucose using engineered E. coli and S. cerevisiae expressing the dha operon genes (dhaB, dhaT, gdrA, gdrB).
Enantioselective reduction of methyl benzoylformate using Saccharomyces cerevisiae AS2.1392 whole-cell biocatalyst, achieving 103 mmol/L concentration with 85.8% yield and 95.4% enantiomeric excess

Produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing flavonoid glucosyltransferase (DicGT4) from Dianthus caryophyllus using naringenin as substrate. Highest yield of 155 mg/L achieved with orotic acid supplementation.

Produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing flavonoid glucosyltransferase (DicGT4) from Dianthus caryophyllus using naringenin as substrate. Highest yield of 82 mg/L achieved in 2xMSGI medium.

A pharmaceutical intermediate and food additive produced from orotic acid using engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae as a whole-cell biocatalyst, with enhanced production achieved through metabolic flux redistribution.

Ethanol produced by Saccharomyces cerevisiae fermentation of YP/glucose media for use as a biofuel. The study investigates using nisin to control lactobacilli contamination, reducing lactic acid production and increasing ethanol yield.

Lipoic acid (also known as 6,8-thioctic acid, 1,2-dithiolane-3-pentanoic acid) is a naturally occurring cofactor and potent antioxidant. It is an essential cofactor for pyruvate dehydrogenase, α-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase, and glycine cleavage enzyme complexes. It is used in therapies for diseases accompanied by oxidative stress such as diabetes, atherosclerosis, neurodegenerative diseases, and AIDS, and as a detoxication agent for heavy metal poisoning.

Synthesized from sucrose using invertase enzyme from Saccharomyces cerevisiae under free enzyme conditions, with a maximum yield of 10% observed at 525 g/L initial sucrose concentration, 6 U/mL enzyme, pH 5.5, and 40°C. 1-Kestose was the major product among different forms of FOS.

Aromatic compound produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae from glucose via the shikimate pathway; used as a feedstock for liquid crystal polymers and the thermoplastic Vectran™, with applications in space technology due to high tensile strength and chemical resistance.
Commercial wine seed culture produced in aerobic fed-batch cultivation to influence organoleptic properties of wine, with optimized biomass yield and fermentative performance.
Commercial wine seed culture produced in aerobic fed-batch cultivation to influence organoleptic properties of wine, with optimized biomass yield and fermentative performance.
Mentioned as an emerging biodegradable plastic product that relies on lactic acid as a renewable raw material.

Detected as an additional product in bioreactor cultivations of engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain UBR2CBS-DHA-SA-AnDCT-02. Accumulated slowly throughout the cultivation up to approximately 2 g/L.

Recombinant α-amylase from Aspergillus terreus (ateA) produced extracellularly by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains. The enzyme hydrolyzes internal α-1,4-glycosidic bonds in starch to produce maltodextrins, with expression enhanced through promoter engineering and intron addition.
Alcoholic beverage produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain with decreased higher alcohol content via BAT2 gene deletion
Higher alcohol produced from isoleucine degradation via Ehrlich pathway, reduced in BAT2-deletion engineered strains
A higher alcohol produced during fermentation; its concentration decreased in the engineered strains compared to the parental strain.

A renewable polymer derived from lactic acid, produced by engineered microbial cell factories. PLA is used as a sustainable alternative to petroleum-based plastics due to its desirable material properties and biodegradability.
A valuable agent for calming the nerve, synthesized from 2-Phenylethanol.

Inulin is extracted from Jerusalem artichoke tubers (JAT) and can be used as a food ingredient or further modified for biomedical applications. It serves as a feedstock for producing biofuels and other biorefinery products.
A precursor of beta-elemene, produced through metabolic engineering of Saccharomyces cerevisiae by optimizing the mevalonate pathway and enzyme fusion strategies.
A sesquiterpene with antitumor activity, produced from germacrene A through a one-step chemical reaction in vitro.
Produced through metabolic engineering of Saccharomyces cerevisiae, used as precursors for biofuels and oleochemicals.
Cucurbitadienol is the skeleton of cucurbitane-type triterpenoids, used in traditional medicine and as a precursor for mogrosides, which are zero-calorie sweeteners.
A sterol biosynthetic precursor found in Saccharomyces cerevisiae mutants used for sterol intermediate production.
A fructose polymer with diverse applications in food and medical industries, produced by recombinant Saccharomyces cerevisiae hypersecreting bacterial levansucrase.

Recombinant glucoamylase from Aspergillus tubingensis (glaA) co-expressed with α-amylase in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains. The enzyme hydrolyzes terminal 1,4-linked residues to release glucose units for fermentation.
Enzyme produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae with significant activity towards a-1,6-linkages in starch, enhancing complete hydrolysis.

Produced from cellulosic materials using thermotolerant Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain TJ14 via semi-simultaneous saccharification and fermentation (SSSF) at 39°C, yielding 45 g/L ethanol from 100 g/L cellulose.

Recombinant strain S288C-LCB4 produces more trehalose than control strain under inhibitor stress. Trehalose acts as a protective compound that helps maintain cell pH balance and stabilizes macromolecules.

CWC are produced and released into the culture broth by Saccharomyces cerevisiae protoplasts under specific static liquid culture conditions. These components include β-1,3/1,6-glucan and mannan, which have physiological and pharmacological functions, such as activating macrophages and stimulating TNF-α production.
THCA is the precursor of the psychoactive cannabinoid THC, produced via bioconversion of cannabigerolic acid (CBGA) by engineered Pichia pastoris expressing THCA synthase from Cannabis sativa.
Produced through co-culture fermentation of Scheffersomyces stipitis and Saccharomyces cerevisiae, optimized for glucose and xylose conversion.
Amino acid overproduced through metabolic engineering of S. cerevisiae.

High value-added organic acid produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. The strain co-expresses mouse myo-inositol oxygenase (MIOX) and Pseudomonas putida uronate dehydrogenase (Udh), with overexpressed inositol-1-phosphate synthase (INO1) and deleted phosphofructokinase (PFK1), achieving a titer of 230.22 mg/L. Used in food, pharmaceutical, and chemical industries for cholesterol reduction, cancer treatment, and polymer synthesis.
Fatty alcohol produced from xylose using engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae, with applications in detergents, emulsifiers, lubricants, cosmetics, and biofuels.
Odd-chain fatty alcohols are produced by E. coli through a novel pathway involving α-dioxygenase, yielding C11-C13 alcohols for specialized applications.
Precursor of vitamin D4, produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through metabolic engineering of the ergosterol pathway, specifically by disrupting the erg5 gene.
Highest ester concentration detected (68.7 mg L−1), predominantly produced in tequila vinasse by non-Saccharomyces yeast strains like Candida parapsilosis CCMA 0544.
Primary ABE solvent produced by Clostridium acetobutylicum; production reduced in engineered Cac-NOX strain due to NADH oxidation.
Byproduct formed during molasses fermentation by Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Yield minimized to 3.34 g per 100 L fermentation broth under optimized conditions (29°Brix, pH 4.5, 30% inoculum).
A pentose sugar released during the hydrolysis of hemicellulose in lignocellulosic biomass, part of the fermentable sugars.
Produced via enzymatic hydrolysis of cellulose from detoxified lignocellulosic biomass, with conversion rates up to 94%.
A pentose sugar released during the hydrolysis of hemicellulose, detected in the hydrolysate.
A hexose sugar present in the hydrolysate, part of the combined sugar fraction analyzed during fermentation.
A hexose sugar consumed by Saccharomyces cerevisiae D5A during fermentation in the absence of glucose, contributing to ethanol production.
Furan aldehyde formed by degradation of hexoses during prehydrolysis, more prevalent in rice hull and peanut shell prehydrolysates.
Derived from partial degradation of lignin or hemicellulose substituents, most abundant in prehydrolysates of bagasse and rice hulls.
Alkaloid secondary metabolite produced by engineered yeast strains through transfer of Ephedra genomic DNA via ion implantation.
Alkaloid secondary metabolite produced by engineered yeast strains through transfer of Ephedra genomic DNA via ion implantation.
Industrial enzymes produced from DDGS-derived sugars.
Enzyme produced by Kluyveromyces marxianus MTCC 1389 using whey as a substrate, catalyzing the hydrolysis of lactose into glucose and galactose.

A non-proteinogenic amino acid acting as a major regulator in the central nervous system, used in pharmaceuticals, functional foods, and as a building block for bio-plastics. Produced via enzymatic conversion of L-glutamic acid by glutamate decarboxylase (GAD) expressed in recombinant E. coli.
Alcoholic beverage produced from the fermentation of a diluted honey solution, with an alcoholic strength between 8 and 18% v v−1.
An oleanane-type pentacyclic triterpene with applications in food and healthcare, exhibiting antiviral, anti-inflammatory, neuroprotective, antiobesity, antidiabetic, and anticancer effects.
A flavor ester synthesized by esterase from Candida parapsilosis expressed in Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Used in the food industry for its characteristic flavor.

Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae produces isobutyl acetate at 260.2 mg/L. This medium branched-chain ester serves as biofuel, flavoring agent, and eco-friendly toluene alternative in coatings, inks, fragrances, and cosmetics.
Reducing sugars released during enzymatic hydrolysis of cassava pulp, with concentrations ranging from 488.8 to 707.1 mg/g biomass depending on initial solid loading.
Produced by Pleurotus ostreatus HP-1 during fungal pretreatment to degrade lignin in lignocellulosic biomass.
Produced alongside cellulases by Trichoderma reesei, used in the hydrolysis of hemicellulose components in Kans grass.
Used in enzymatic hydrolysis to convert cellobiose into glucose for fermentation.
Produced in Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains CEN.PK and S288c, with higher production in S288c.

A biodegradable polyester synthesized by engineered Pseudomonas putida KT2440-acs using starch as the initial carbon source, converted to glucose and then acetic acid. It consists of monomers 3-hydroxydecanoate (C10) and 3-hydroxyoctanoate (C8), with potential applications in medical devices and biodegradable plastics.
Produced during enzymatic hydrolysis of pretreated sugarcane bagasse, primarily consisting of glucose and other reducing sugars.
Extracted during pretreatment of sugarcane bagasse, contributing to the sugar content in hydrolysates.
A distilled alcoholic beverage produced from fermented chestnut using Aspergillus oryzae and Saccharomyces cerevisiae, following a traditional Japanese fermentation process.
A flavonoid glycoside derivative of liquiritigenin, used in Chinese medicine for its anti-cancer and anti-inflammatory effects.
A glycoside derivative of isoliquiritigenin, known for inhibiting tumor angiogenesis and showing antidepressant effects.
A dihydroflavone compound formed by the isomerization of isoliquiritigenin, with anti-cancer properties.
A chalcone with significant pharmacological activities including anti-inflammatory, anticancer, and antioxidant effects.
A chalcone intermediate in the flavonoid biosynthesis pathway.
Optically pure l(+)-lactic acid produced by metabolically engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae OC-2T T165R, immobilized in photo-crosslinkable resin gels for enhanced fermentation performance.
Biodegradable polymer improved in thermostability by incorporating D-lactic acid as a monomer.
Enzyme optimized for high catalytic efficiency in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae to produce D-lactic acid.

Produced through co-culture fermentation of hexose-fermenting yeasts (Saccharomyces cerevisiae) and xylose-fermenting yeasts (Scheffersomyces stipitis, Spathaspora passalidarum) using a mixture of sugarcane molasses and bagasse hydrolysate as substrate.
Monoterpene produced by engineered yeast; precursor to oxidized monoterpenoids like trans-sabin-3-ol.
Lipid-derived products produced in Yarrowia lipolytica by subcellular engineering of lipase-dependent pathways directed towards lipid-related organelles.
Produced by fermentation of spent sulfite liquor (SSL) using genetically modified Saccharomyces cerevisiae 259ST, which is engineered to ferment xylose in addition to hexose sugars.
Conversion of HMF (5-hydroxymethyl-2-furaldehyde) to its corresponding alcohol during fermentation, indicating detoxification activity.

Byproduct of malic acid production by engineered S. cerevisiae strain RWB525. Produced at concentrations up to 12 g/L (partially consumed after glucose depletion). Key precursor for malate synthesis that accumulates due to pathway kinetic limitations.

Enantiopure (R)-PED produced via asymmetric reduction of 2-hydroxy-1-phenylethanone (HPE) by Saccharomyces cerevisiae JUC15, achieving >99.9% enantiomeric excess (e.e.) and high yield (71% at 20 g/L substrate concentration). The engineered yeast exhibits remarkable operational stability, reusability for 40 cycles, and functions over a wide pH range (4-9).
Produced from sorghum stover using Saccharomyces cerevisiae and Pachysolen tannophilus under optimized conditions, achieving yields of 68 g/L and 56 g/L respectively.
A degradation product of hexose sugars, removed during detoxification to improve fermentation efficiency.

Produced by red yeasts (Rhodotorula glutinis, Cystofilobasidium capitatum, Sporobolomyces roseus) using hydrothermally pretreated wheat straw hydrolysates. S. roseus showed the highest production of β-carotene (1.23-1.56 mg g⁻¹) and total carotenoids.
Organic acid overproduced via metabolic engineering of S. cerevisiae.
Produced by anaerobic digestion of whole stillage (residue from ethanol fermentation) in a 3-L anaerobic digester. Methane yield: 96 g/kg corn stover; productivity: 320 mL CH₄/g volatile solids.
Biopolymer produced by fermenting non-detoxified rice straw hydrolyzate with Comamonas sp., achieving 35.86% yield without nutrient supplementation.
Solid residues after filtration containing up to 32% crude protein, reduced antinutritionals (e.g., condensed tannins), and enriched fiber content, used as animal feed.
Neurospora intermedia biomass cultivated on thin stillage, containing over 62% crude protein and suitable as a feed additive.
Produced from xylose in residual lignocellulosic hydrolysate via aerobic fermentation. Contains 54.6% crude protein and all essential amino acids except cysteine, suitable for animal feed.
Recovered from alkaline pre-extraction liquor as high-purity (99.8%) amorphous particles; 86.4% of initial silica in wheat straw was recovered.
Produced by transesterification of lipids extracted from microalgae biomass grown in dairy wastewater. Composed of saturated and unsaturated fatty acids suitable for biodiesel applications.

Renewable fuel produced via co-culture fermentation of Saccharomyces cerevisiae and Pichia sp. using pretreated rice straw hydrolysate. Achieved concentrations of 4.32 g/L at lab scale and 13.3 g/L in scale-up experiments.

Manufactured by extruding residual bagasse (after pretreatment) with poly(lactic acid) (PLA) in a 2:1 PLA:fiber ratio. Exhibits tensile strength of 49.5 MPa and flexible strength of 65 MPa, designed for biodegradable applications.

fields.grna_map: CRISPR with gRNA used (sequences in Table S3)
fields.description: Fermentation broth containing 2,3-butanediol produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae, used without purification to induce drought tolerance in plants such as Arabidopsis thaliana.
A non-terpenoid mono-unsaturated fatty acid co-overproduced (154.94 mg/L) in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain FOH-2 due to indirect upregulation by ergosterol overproduction.

A tricyclic sesquiterpene used in fragrances, perfumes, resins, flotation agents, and as a biofuel substitute for aviation kerosene. Produced via engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through metabolic engineering strategies including MVA pathway optimization, competitive pathway elimination, molecular chaperone screening, and acetyl-CoA supply enhancement.
Redox-active lipid essential for mitochondrial electron transport and antioxidant functions, produced by engineered yeast strains including conditional mutants like Gal-YAH1 and Gal-ARH1.

Synthesized from 5-hydroxymethylfurfural (HMF) via biocatalytic reduction using recombinant Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing aryl alcohol dehydrogenase MgAAD1669. Achieves 94% yield and 99% selectivity at 250 mM substrate concentration. Fed-batch strategy enables space-time yield of 3.4 g/L·h.

Key aroma compound in Sauvignon Blanc wines, contributes black currant, boxwood, and cat urine notes. Produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains via overexpression of the IRC7 gene or bacterial TnaA gene.

Key aroma compound in Sauvignon Blanc wines, provides grapefruit (R-enantiomer) and passionfruit (S-enantiomer) notes. Enhanced through overexpression of IRC7 or TnaA genes in Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

Acetylated derivative of 3MH, contributes passionfruit (R-enantiomer) and sweaty/cat urine notes (S-enantiomer) to wine. Production increased via IRC7 or TnaA overexpression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

A natural phenolic compound with strong antioxidant activity, produced via an artificial pathway in engineered E. coli. Achieved titers of 1243 ± 165 mg/L from tyrosine feeding and 647±35 mg/L from simple carbon sources (e.g., glucose/glycerol) using enzyme optimization (Aro10, ADH6, HpaBC), feaB knockout, NH4Cl removal, ascorbic acid addition, and biphasic extraction with 1-dodecanol.

Primary compound and intermediate precursor for various chemical products, produced via biotransformation of lauric acid using engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing CYP52A17SS. Highest yield achieved was 20.8 μM at 72 hours in culture.
Intermediate hydroxylated fatty acid produced during the ω-oxidation of lauric acid by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing CYP52A17SS. Highest yield was 45.8 μM at 24 hours in culture.
Ester with floral, honey, or sweet flavor. Lower in mixed fermentations.
Carboxylic acid. Significantly lower in mixed fermentations.
Polyol produced by filamentous fungi during starch hydrolysis in Baijiu fermentation; inhibits ethanol production in Saccharomyces cerevisiae and Pichia kudriavzevii.
Polyol generated by filamentous fungi during starch saccharification; reduces ethanol yield in functional yeasts during Baijiu production.

Unnatural chiral amino acid used as a precursor for angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitors to treat hypertension and cardiovascular diseases. Produced via fermentation using recombinant Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing E. coli aspartate aminotransferase, achieving 43.7 mM yield and 78.9% productivity improvement over wild-type.

Produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains via metabolic engineering. Accumulation of 650 μM achieved using S. cerevisiae S288C Δaro7 with plasmid pCV3-UBIC expressing E. coli ubiC gene (chorismate lyase). Theoretical mass yield: 0.58 g/g glucose.

Produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains via metabolic engineering. Accumulation of 250 μM achieved using S. cerevisiae S288C Δaro7 Δtrp3 with plasmid pCV3-ABZ1 expressing abz1 gene from wine yeast AWRI1631 (para-aminobenzoate synthase). Theoretical mass yield: 0.53 g/g glucose.

Produced by metabolically engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae overexpressing ERG10 (acetyl-CoA C-acetyltransferase), hbd (acetoacetyl-CoA reductase), and tesB (3-hydroxybutyryl-CoA thioesterase) genes. The engineered yeast utilizes ethanol as a carbon source to accumulate cytosolic acetyl-CoA for S-3HB biosynthesis. A final titer of 12.0 g/L was achieved through fed-batch fermentation with ethanol pulse-feeding. This bio-based platform chemical serves as a precursor for biodegradable polymers (polyhydroxyalkanoates) and stereo-specific fine chemicals including antibiotics, pheromones, and drugs.

Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae produces 3-methyl-1-butyl acetate at 296.1 mg/L. This medium branched-chain ester functions as biofuel, banana-flavored food additive, and green solvent in coatings and pharmaceuticals.
Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae produces 2-methyl-1-butyl acetate at 289.6 mg/L. This medium branched-chain ester is used as biofuel and industrial chemical.

L-PAC is an enantiopure α-hydroxy ketone produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through biotransformation of benzaldehyde. The ∆zwf1 deletion strain achieved the highest production of 2.48 g/L from 2 g/L benzaldehyde, reaching 88% of theoretical yield. L-PAC serves as a key precursor for pharmaceutical synthesis of decongestant, antiasthmatic and bronchodilator medicines including ephedrine and pseudoephedrine.

S-adenosyl-L-methionine (SAM) produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through spaceflight mutagenesis, overexpression of heterologous methionine adenosyltransferase (MAT2), and optimized cultivation conditions. Final production reached 7.76 g/L in shake flasks and 9.64 g/L in 50-L fermenter.

A natural flavonoid produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via metabolic engineering of the fatty acid β-oxidation pathway to enhance acetyl-CoA supply. Achieved a titer of 1129.44 mg/L in fed-batch fermentation, the highest reported in any microorganism.
Byproduct (4-hydroxymandelic acid) produced by hydroxymandelate synthase from hydroxyphenylpyruvate, the preferred substrate. Competes with mandelic acid production in engineered yeast strains.
Ehrlich pathway product produced from phenylpyruvate in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains. Co-produced with phenylethanol as a byproduct of aromatic amino acid metabolism.

fields.grna_map: URA3-targeting gRNA for CRISPR/Cas9-mediated genomic integration
fields.description: De novo production of m-cresol (3-methylphenol) from glucose in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via heterologous expression of 6-methylsalicylic acid synthase and decarboxylase. Achieved titers up to 589 mg/L in complex medium. Valuable platform chemical for flavors, disinfectants, antiseptics, antioxidants, and menthol synthesis.

fields.grna_map: URA3-targeting gRNA for CRISPR/Cas9-mediated genomic integration
fields.description: Polyketide intermediate produced by heterologous 6-methylsalicylic acid synthase in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Genomic integration enabled titers exceeding 2 g/L, serving as direct precursor for m-cresol conversion.

Medium-chain ω-hydroxy fatty acid produced de novo from glucose or ethanol in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae by combining mutated fatty acid synthase (FAS1R1834K/FAS2) with cytochrome P450 (CYP539A7-cprA). Achieved titers up to 3 mg/L.

fields.grna_map: CRISPR-Cas9 with guide RNA targeting ILV2 locus for pathway integration
fields.description: By-product formed by oxidation of isobutyraldehyde under aerobic conditions. Produced at yields of 0.0657 ± 0.005 mol/mol glucose in micro-aerobic cultures, exceeding isobutanol yields.

An intermediate metabolite produced from α-acetolactate and reduced to acetoin by BDH enzymes. Content was measured in engineered strains (30.85-34.06 mg/L range) but overexpression of ILV2 gene did not significantly increase its production.

Engineered S. cerevisiae strains accumulated triacylglycerols (TAGs) to more than 17% of dry cell weight, a four-fold improvement over control strains, representing the highest lipid content achieved in this study.

S-licarbazepine is produced by asymmetric reduction of oxcarbazepine using Saccharomyces cerevisiae CGMCC No. 2266. It serves as a chiral intermediate for eslicarbazepine acetate, a CNS-active anticonvulsant for epilepsy and bipolar disorder treatment. The process achieves 100% enantiomeric excess with both batch and continuous reduction, yielding 3.7678 mmol L−1 d−1 in continuous operation.

An optically active chiral synthon used in the pharmaceutical industry, produced via asymmetric bioreduction of ethyl 4-chloro acetoacetate using Saccharomyces cerevisiae (bakers' yeast). Optimization of culture medium and reaction conditions improved enantiomeric excess from 82.1% to 92.3% and yield from 77.3% to 82.3%.
A glycosylated triterpenoid intermediate in CK biosynthesis, produced at 0.82 g/L by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
A pentacyclic triterpenoid saponin produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae at 5.98 ± 0.47 mg/L. Key licorice compound with pharmacological activities and sweetener applications.
A triterpenoid glycoside intermediate produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae at 2.31 ± 0.21 mg/L.

Total sesquiterpenes produced as a mixture by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing the multi-product enzyme cubebol synthase. The total titer includes cubebol and other sesquiterpene by-products. Metabolic engineering of nitrogen assimilation (GDH1 deletion/GDH2 overexpression) and mevalonate pathway (tHMG1 overexpression) enhanced production to a maximum of 30.1 mg/L in batch fermentation.

An isoflavone produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain GEN23 expressing 7 plant genes (PAL, C4H, CPR, 4CL, CHS, CHI, IFS). Production varied from 0.1 mg/L (from phenylalanine) to 7.7 mg/L (from naringenin precursor) in defined media.

Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae producing dihydroxyacetone from glucose via glycerol intermediate. The best performing strain overexpresses Hansenula polymorpha glycerol dehydrogenase and has DAK1/DAK2 double deletion, achieving 700 mg/l DHA from 20 g/l glucose under osmotic stress conditions.
A secondary sesquiterpene byproduct formed by the santalene synthase enzyme, representing approximately 12% of the total sesquiterpene production alongside α-santalene.

A sesquiterpenoid alcohol byproduct formed by dephosphorylation of farnesyl diphosphate (FPP) when santalene synthase capacity is saturated. Accumulates when ERG9 is down-regulated and FPP pool increases, serving as an overflow metabolite.

Extracellular free fatty acids (FFAs) produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through systematic disruption of β-oxidation (Δfaa2 Δpxa1 Δpox1) and acyl-CoA synthetase genes (Δfaa1 Δfaa4 Δfat1), combined with overexpression of DGA1 (diacylglycerol acyltransferase) and TGL3 (triacylglycerol lipase) to enhance neutral lipid recycling. The engineered sextuple mutant strain achieved 2.2 g/L extracellular FFAs after 48 h, representing a 4.2-fold increase over previously reported levels for S. cerevisiae and reaching 32% of theoretical maximum yield.

Production of fatty acid ethyl esters (FAEEs, biodiesel) at approximately 5 mg/L directly from simple sugars, achieved by heterologous expression of wax ester synthase from A. calcoaceticus in engineered S. cerevisiae.

Enantiopure (2R,3R)-butanediol (R-BDO) produced at high titer (>100 g/L) and yield (>70% theoretical) from glucose and galactose mixtures using engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae with deleted pyruvate decarboxylases and overexpressed BDO biosynthetic pathway.

Triacetic acid lactone (TAL) is a polyketide biorenewable chemical precursor produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. The study achieved a final titer of 1.6 g/L (35% of theoretical yield, 0.16 g/g glucose) by combining a NADPH-generating modified E. coli pyruvate dehydrogenase complex (PDHm) with ZWF1 deletion and mitochondrial transport gene deletions (POR2, MPC2, PDA1, YAT2) to enhance cytosolic acetyl-CoA availability for polyketide synthesis.
Specific carotenoid comprising 32.56% of total carotenoid production. Beta-carotene is a vitamin A precursor and antioxidant pigment.

Protoberberine alkaloid produced from dopamine via reticuline using co-cultures of transgenic Escherichia coli and Saccharomyces cerevisiae cells. Final yield of 8.3 mg/liter culture medium within 48 hours.
A protoberberine alkaloid produced in engineered yeast at up to 219 µg/L via heterologous expression of cheilanthifoline synthase cytochrome P450 with optimized CPR pairing and promoter selection

A protoberberine alkaloid produced in engineered yeast at 676 µg/L through multi-enzyme pathway optimization including temperature reduction, two-stage culture, and galactose feeding, representing the highest microbial titer reported
An N-methylated protoberberine intermediate produced in engineered yeast at 548 µg/L with 77% conversion efficiency through expression of tetrahydroprotoberberine N-methyltransferase
A protopine alkaloid produced in engineered yeast at 252 µg/L through expression of cis-N-methylstylopine 14-hydroxylase (MSH) cytochrome P450, converting the protoberberine to protopine scaffold
The oxidized derivative of dihydrosanguinarine, produced spontaneously ex vivo. It is a BIA with recognized antimicrobial activities and potential as an antineoplastic drug.

Short branched-chain fatty acid (C4) produced via engineered Ehrlich pathway in Saccharomyces cerevisiae by combinatorial overexpression of key genes (BAT1, ARO10, ALD2, ALD5) and deletion of competing alcohol dehydrogenases. Platform chemical for synthesizing plasticizers, surfactants, textile auxiliaries, fragrances, flavorings, and methacrylic acid.

Short branched-chain fatty acid (C5) produced via engineered Ehrlich pathway in Saccharomyces cerevisiae by combinatorial overexpression of key genes (BAT1, ARO10, ALD2, ALD5) and deletion of competing alcohol dehydrogenases. Platform chemical for synthesizing sedatives, fragrances, and anticonvulsant drug derivatives.

Short branched-chain fatty acid (C5) produced via engineered Ehrlich pathway in Saccharomyces cerevisiae by combinatorial overexpression of key genes (BAT1, ARO10, ALD2, ALD5) and deletion of competing alcohol dehydrogenases. Platform chemical for flavorings and pharmaceutical drugs including statins (mevastatin, pravastatin, lovastatin).

De novo biosynthesis of caffeine in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae achieved through heterologous expression of N-methyltransferases from Coffea arabica. Production titers reached 274 μg/L in 0.3-L bench-scale batch fermentations through optimization of pathway flux, enzyme copy number, and codon optimization.

Selective production of 3-methylxanthine as a monomethylated xanthine with high specificity and titers of 3700-3710 μg/L in bench-scale fermentations. Represents a non-accumulating intermediate in natural plants that is challenging to obtain through chemical synthesis.

Key intermediate in caffeine biosynthesis pathway, produced through engineered xanthine-to-xanthosine conversion in yeast central metabolism. Initial production of 2.3 μg/L was improved nearly 8-fold through metabolic engineering of purine flux.

Acyclic sesquiterpene alcohol produced in Saccharomyces cerevisiae through metabolic engineering of the mevalonate pathway and destabilization of squalene synthase. Final titre of ~100 mg/L achieved in strain N6D. Used as fragrance and flavor, with antiulcer, antileishmanial, and insect repelling activities. Serves as intermediate for vitamins E/K, α-sinensal, and teprenone synthesis.

A prenyl alcohol that accumulates as a byproduct in engineered yeast during casbene production, formed by endogenous phosphatases acting on excess geranylgeranyl diphosphate. Accumulates up to 79.9 mg/L in optimized strains.

Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae produces fatty alcohols via metabolic engineering including heterologous expression of Mus musculus fatty acid reductase (FAR), overexpression of ACC1**, and deletion of competing pathways. Highest titer: 6.0 g/L in fed-batch fermentation.
Formed during fermentation; differences in profiles noted with SO2 treatment, but final concentrations similar.
Seco-iridoid produced in vitro via Cr10HGO-CrIS enzyme pair activity. Key precursor for monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis.
Undesired saturated shunt product formed during iridoid biosynthesis; represents carbon loss in metabolic pathway.

Produced from glycerol via modular metabolic engineering in Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Involves heterologous methylglyoxal pathway enzymes (E. coli mgsA, gldA, yqhD), glycerol facilitator expression (CjFPS1), replacement of native glycerol catabolism with NAD⁺-dependent DHA pathway, and downregulation of triosephosphate isomerase (TPI1). Achieved titers >4 g/L in bioreactors.

Produced in metabolically engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via an integrated strategy including enzyme discovery, pathway optimization, and fusion protein construction. The production reached 8.78 mg/L in fed-batch fermentation, a 630-fold increase over the parent strain.

Produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae using optimized CYP450/CPR pairing. Achieved titer of 108.1 ± 4.6 mg/L. Serves as a precursor for triterpenoid synthesis and exhibits anti-proliferative and anti-inflammatory activities.
Lathyrane diterpenoid precursor for anti-cancer and anti-HIV drugs like ingenol-3-angelate and prostratin, produced at ~800 mg/L in engineered yeast.
Singly oxidized casbene derivative produced by C9OX cytochrome P450s (e.g., ElCYP71D445), reaching 44 mg/L.
Oxidized casbene derivative generated by CYP activity, often co-produced with 9-hydroxycasbene.
Doubly oxidized intermediate formed by JcCYP726A20 activity, quantified via HPLC/NMR.
Triply oxidized casbane intermediate preceding jolkinol C formation, accumulated in ADH-engineered strains.
Orange betalain pigment synthesized via condensation of betalamic acid with para-aminobenzoic acid in engineered S. cerevisiae.
Building block for aromatic drugs and flavors; derivatives exhibit high antioxidative activity and can be converted to flavor compounds like ethyl vanillin.
Sesquiterpenoid precursor to valerenic acid produced via engineered mevalonate pathway in yeast.
Sedative and anxiolytic sesquiterpenoid compound engineered in yeast via P450 oxidation and dehydrogenase activity.
Produced from D-xylose using native glycolytic enzymes (phosphofructokinase and fructose-bisphosphate aldolase) in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain yEG04-P

Anthraquinone produced via engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing TE-less NR-PKS (SlACAS) and MβL-TE (HyTE), optimized with ACC1 mutation and pyruvate carbon source. Highest titer: 661.2 mg/L in fed-batch fermentation.

Anthraquinone produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through decarboxylation of endocrocin precursor using AfDC enzyme, with ACC1 mutation and carbon source optimization. Highest titer: 528.4 mg/L in fed-batch fermentation.
Hydroxylated monoterpenoid intermediate produced via CrG8H catalysis, achieved 227 mg/L in fed-batch fermentation with quantitative conversion from geraniol.

De novo production in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via a heterologous pathway from Psilocybe cubensis. Titers reached 627 mg/L in fed-batch fermentation. It is a tryptamine-derived psychoactive alkaloid with pharmaceutical applications.

Dephosphorylated degradation product of psilocybin, produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains. Titers reached 580 mg/L in fed-batch fermentation. It is the bioactive form that binds to serotonin receptors.
Natural tryptamine derivative (tri-N-methylated) produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. The dephosphorylated form was detected, indicating production and subsequent dephosphorylation.
New-to-nature tryptamine derivative produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing a serotonin N-acetyltransferase from Bos taurus that acetylates 4-hydroxytryptamine.

Epoxycarotenoid produced via heterologous expression of β-carotene hydroxylase (CrtZ) and zeaxanthin epoxidase (ZEP) in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Achieved through multi-level strategies including enzyme truncation, redox partner co-expression, and gene dosage optimization.
Flavonoid with antioxidative, antihyperlipidemic, hepatoprotective, and neuroprotective activities; used in dietary supplements and functional foods.
Phenylpropanoid precursor for lignans (e.g., pinoresinol, podophyllotoxin, etoposide) and flavonolignans.
Major flavonolignan constituent of silymarin; used globally in herbal medicines (e.g., Legalon®, Silipide®) for treating hepatic diseases like viral hepatitis and cirrhosis.
Major flavonolignan constituent of silymarin; used alongside silybin in herbal medicines for liver disorders.

Human milk oligosaccharide produced via salvage pathway in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae using fucose and lactose as substrates. Achieved titers of 92 mg/L (batch fermentation) and 503 mg/L (fed-batch fermentation).

Isoprenol (3-methyl-3-butene-1-ol) is engineered in Saccharomyces cerevisiae as a drop-in biofuel and precursor for commodity chemicals, achieving titers up to 383.1 mg/L via mevalonate and IPP-bypass pathways with phosphatase optimization.

A chiral compound with a buttery flavor used as a taste and fragrance enhancer in food and cosmetics industries. Also serves as a building block for valuable chemicals like acetylbutanediol and alkyl pyrazines. Produced via engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae with high yield (96% theoretical) and stereospecificity (98.2%).

Produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae by introducing PgUGT71A53 and PgUGT71A54 into a PPT-producing chassis strain. It is a major protopanaxatriol-type saponin with cardiovascular, neuroprotective, endocrine, and immune protective activities.

Produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae by introducing PgUGT94Q13 and a heterologous UDP-xylose biosynthetic pathway (from Arabidopsis thaliana) into an Rg1-producing strain. It is a bioactive compound regulating microcirculation and hemostasis.

Produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae by introducing PgUGT71A54, PgUGT94Q13, and a heterologous UDP-xylose biosynthetic pathway into a PPT-producing chassis strain. It is a bioactive compound regulating microcirculation and hemostasis.

An exopolysaccharide produced by Lactobacillus kefiranofaciens, containing approximately equal amounts of glucose and galactose. Enhanced production achieved via mixed culture with Saccharomyces cerevisiae, which consumes inhibitory lactic acid and stimulates bacterial growth.
Antithrombotic protein produced by recombinant Saccharomyces cerevisiae with enhanced secretion via BiP coexpression.

Vitamin E isomer produced de novo from glucose in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via heterologous pathway integration and metabolic engineering. Achieved titers up to 4.10 mg/L in fed-batch fermentation.

Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains produce DHM, a flavonoid with antioxidant, anti-tumor, and neuroprotective properties, through metabolic engineering including coexpression of hydroxylases (F3H, F3'5'H) and cytochrome P450 reductases (CPR).

De novo biosynthesis in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae from glucose via metabolic engineering and fermentation optimization. Achieved 545 mg/L in fermenter. Exhibits anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardioprotective effects, with superior bioavailability and structural stability compared to resveratrol.

De novo biosynthesis in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae by enhancing precursor supply, constructing fusion proteins, and using the vacuolar transporter BPT1 to facilitate CBGA transport for conversion by CBDAS. Achieved a yield of 6.92 mg/L in fed-batch fermentation.
A monosaccharide produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through metabolic engineering strategies to reduce glucose metabolic rate, used in dietary supplements and therapeutic agents.
A rare hexoketose used as a low-calorie sweetener and functional food additive, produced from lactose in cheese whey powder via enzymatic hydrolysis and isomerization.
Intermediate in kaempferol biosynthesis pathway produced by engineered S. cerevisiae via F3H catalysis.

Prebiotic sugar produced from lactose using recombinant cellobiose 2-epimerase expressed in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Demonstrates prebiotic effects, anti-carcinogenic, anti-obesity, and hypocholesterolemic properties.
Prebiotic sugar produced as a by-product from lactose via enzymatic isomerization using cellobiose 2-epimerase expressed in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
Accumulates to 1.68 g/L during wheat fermentation using Saccharomyces cerevisiae, used as a nootropic for cognitive health and in cosmetics.

fields.grna_map: gRNA helper vectors used in EasyClone Markerfree toolkit for CRISPR/Cas9 integration (Supplementary Figure S3, N)
fields.description: Hydroxycinnamic acid ester produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via metabolic engineering of aromatic amino acid pathways and heterologous expression of plant/E. coli enzymes. Achieved titers up to 5.93 ± 0.06 mg/L in mineral medium with glucose.
Produced de novo in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via hydroxylation of chrysin at the C6 position using SbaiCYP82D-4 enzyme. Titer reached 4.69 mg/L in SD medium.
Synthesized de novo in yeast by glycosylation of baicalein using EbF7GAT and EbUDPGDH enzymes. Achieved a titer of 5.53 mg/L in SD medium.
Produced de novo via hydroxylation of chrysin at the C8 position using EgutCYP82D-2 enzyme. Yield reached 7.05 mg/L in SD medium.
Generated by methylation of norwogonin at the C8 position using SbarF8OMT enzyme. Titer achieved was 4.43 mg/L in SD medium.
Synthesized de novo via methylation of norwogonin at the C7 position using SbarF7OMT enzyme. Production titer was 4.21 mg/L in SD medium.
Produced by dual methylation of norwogonin at C7 and C8 positions using SbarF7OMT and SbarF8OMT enzymes. Yield reached 3.05 mg/L in SD medium.

A flavonoid produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae from (2S)-naringenin using optimized SmF3′H and SmCPR enzymes, achieving a titer of 3.3 g/L. It exhibits health benefits such as myocardial protective, anti-inflammatory, antiapoptotic, and antioxidant effects, and serves as a precursor for plant natural products like taxifolin and silybin.

A phenolic compound produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae, with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, antiviral, antilipidemic, antiobesity, antidiabetic, antihypertensive, antitumor, hepatoprotective, and neuroprotective activities. Used in food and pharmaceutical industries as a nutraceutical and food additive.
Dihydrochalcone by-product identified in culture supernatant at ~9 mg/L from engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing PAL, 4CL, and CHS.

A sesquiterpene lactone and direct precursor for the anti-glioblastoma drug ACT001, with anti-cancer properties. Produced de novo in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via heterologous expression of germacrene A synthase (GAS) and three cytochrome P450 enzymes (GAO, COS, PTS).
A sesquiterpene lactone intermediate in the parthenolide biosynthesis pathway, produced via oxidation of germacrene A acid by costunolide synthase (COS) in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
Critical MIA pathway intermediate produced at 5.3 mg/L via cytochrome P450 monooxygenase CrG10H in engineered yeast strains.
Diverted pathway intermediate (5.6 mg/L) formed via hydroxylation of citronellol or reduction of 10-hydroxygeraniol.

Monosaccharide produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through hydrolysis of intracellular 2'-Fucosyllactose via heterologous α-L-fucosidase expression. Extracellular titer reached 0.41 g/L in batch fermentation.
UV-absorbing mycosporine-like amino acid (MAA) used as a natural sunscreen material

Precursor to steviol glucosides, produced de novo from glucose in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via a pathway involving cytochrome P450 enzymes (KO and KAH) and NADPH-dependent cytochrome P450 reductases (CPRs).
Advanced biofuel produced via engineered 2-ketoacid recursive pathway in Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Achieves titers up to 1241 mg/L and 86-93% specificity among branched-chain higher alcohols.
A six-carbon dicarboxylic acid with two conjugated double bonds, used as a platform chemical for nylon, polyurethanes, terephthalic acid, and functional copolymers in plastics.

Produced from sugar beet molasses using engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae ATCC 36858, which selectively ferments glucose to ethanol, yielding syrups with >95% fructose content when initial sugar concentration is below 242.0 g/L.
Branched-chain higher alcohol (isobutanol) produced by yeast during high-sugar must fermentation, with concentrations ranging from 29.3 to 45.0 mg/L depending on strain
Ginsenoside compound K (CK) produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae, exhibiting anti-cancer, anti-inflammatory, and anti-allergic properties.
Produced via co-culture of engineered E. coli (taxadiene producer) and S. cerevisiae (expressing cytochrome P450s). Precursors for the anti-cancer drug paclitaxel.
Monoacetylated dioxygenated taxane produced by co-culture. Key intermediate in paclitaxel biosynthesis, synthesized from xylose.

A diterpenoid metabolite produced heterologously in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae yeast strains at titers up to 10.5 mg/L. This bioactive natural product exhibits antibiotic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties and serves as an intermediate in tanshinone biosynthesis.
Functionalized sesquiterpene fragrance molecule produced via co-culture of E. coli (valencene producer) and S. cerevisiae (expressing CYPs and alcohol dehydrogenase).
Key branch point benzylisoquinoline alkaloid produced from norlaudanosoline via engineered methyltransferases in Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
Protoberberine alkaloid synthesized from (S)-reticuline using berberine bridge enzyme (BBE) in engineered yeast.
