Methylated protoberberine intermediate produced from (S)-scoulerine via scoulerine 9-O-methyltransferase (SMT) in yeast.
Berberine pathway intermediate generated from (S)-tetrahydrocolumbamine by cytochrome P450 (CYP719A1) in engineered yeast.
Morphinan alkaloid precursor synthesized from (R)-reticuline using human cytochrome P450 CYP2D6 in engineered yeast.
Natural opiate antitussive produced from thebaine via codeinone reduction by COR in engineered yeast.
Semisynthetic opioid analgesic produced from thebaine using engineered yeast expressing T6ODM and bacterial morB enzyme.
Semisynthetic opioid analgesic produced from thebaine via spontaneous hydroxylation and morB reduction in engineered yeast.
Semisynthetic opioid analgesic produced from morphine via morA and morB enzymatic transformations in engineered yeast.
Opiate isomer byproduct generated from neopine demethylation by CODM in engineered yeast.
Anticancer alkaloid produced via a 14-step biosynthetic pathway from norlaudanosoline in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing 16 heterologous plant enzymes.
Pathway intermediate in noscapine biosynthesis, produced in yeast via enzymatic conversion by PsCXE1 and PsSDR1.
Novel protoberberine alkaloid synthesized in yeast engineered to express CYP82Y1, not naturally found in plants.
Novel protoberberine derivative produced in yeast strains expressing TNMT and CYP82X2.
Reactive textile dye bioaccumulated by Saccharomyces cerevisiae for wastewater treatment.
Reactive textile dye bioaccumulated by Saccharomyces cerevisiae for wastewater treatment.
Reactive textile dye bioaccumulated by Saccharomyces cerevisiae for wastewater treatment.
A biosynthetic alternative to D2 diesel fuel, identified via chemical hydrogenation of bisabolene. It has a cetane number within the range of D2 diesel and superior cold properties.

The fully reduced form of the linear terpene farnesene, mentioned as an alternative biosynthetic diesel being pursued in the market. It was also a byproduct in the hydrogenation of commercial bisabolene.

A saturated very-long-chain fatty alcohol (C22H46O) used as an emollient, emulsifier, thickener in cosmetics and nutritional supplements, and approved as a pharmaceutical antiviral agent for reducing the duration of cold sores caused by the herpes simplex virus.
Fatty acids with chain lengths of 22-26 carbons (e.g., C22:0, C24:0, C26:0), which are essential biological components and precursors for valuable chemicals like fatty alcohols and waxes.
Alcohols derived from VLCFAs, such as docosanol, used in various industrial applications including lubricants, detergents, and coatings.

A plant benzylisoquinoline alkaloid (BIA) produced from (R,S)-norlaudanosoline in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via a reconstituted 10-gene pathway. Dihydrosanguinarine is the immediate precursor to sanguinarine.

A side-product (quaternary BIA) synthesized by the promiscuous activity of tetrahydroprotoberberine cis-N-methyltransferase (TNMT) on scoulerine. Also known as cyclanoline, an acetylcholinesterase inhibitor.
A side-product (quaternary BIA) synthesized by the promiscuous activity of TNMT on cheilanthifoline. This compound had not been previously detected in plants.

An intermediate of L-arginine biosynthesis, used as a dietary supplement and as a precursor for putrescine (a nylon monomer) and tropane alkaloids. Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae produced a titre of 1,041 mg/L in shake-flask cultures and 5.1 g/L in fed-batch cultivations.

A rare ginsenoside with cancer prevention and therapy properties, produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae from glucose via metabolic engineering of the mevalonic acid pathway and optimization of UDP-glycosyltransferase (UGT) activity.
A triterpenoid precursor to PPD, accumulated during the fermentation process and subsequently converted to PPD by engineered P450 enzymes in the yeast chassis.
Monoterpene ether produced by engineered yeast; used in pharmaceuticals and flavors.
Monoterpene produced by engineered yeast; used in synthetic chemistry and as a biofuel.
Monoterpene produced by engineered yeast; used in flavors and fragrances.
Oxidized monoterpenoid produced by engineered yeast via cytochrome P450 oxidation of sabinene.
A key intermediate in the biosynthetic pathway of medicinal tropane alkaloids such as scopolamine, produced de novo from simple carbon and nitrogen sources in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

A non-canonical tropane alkaloid produced de novo by combining biosynthetic modules from distant plant lineages in engineered yeast, demonstrating the platform's utility for discovering TA derivatives.
A key intermediate in the tropane alkaloid biosynthetic pathway, produced from putrescine in engineered yeast through optimization of methyltransferase and oxidase activities.

Aromatic amino acid-derived precursor for many commercially valuable chemicals, produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Achieved a maximum titer of 12.5 g L−1 and a yield of 154.9 mg g−1 on glucose.

Fermentative production in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae by combining heterologous genes from photosynthetic organisms with the endogenous shikimate and mevalonate pathways. Achieved a yield of up to 7.6 mg/g DCW and a titer of 320 mg/L in high-density fermentation using a cold-shock-triggered temperature control system.
A phospholipid derivative with pharmacological applications in ameliorating lipid dysfunction and neurobehavioral symptoms, produced de novo in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
Uncommon polyamine produced by introducing homospermidine synthase into the spermidine platform strain
Tetra-amine polyamine produced by introducing spermine synthase into the engineered yeast platform
Spermine isomer produced by introducing thermospermine synthase (AtACL5) into the yeast platform
N1,N10-bis(glutathionyl)spermidine produced by introducing trypanothione synthetase into the engineered yeast
Polyamine-dihydrocaffeic acid conjugates produced using N-hydrocinnamoyltransferases and CoA ligase systems
Mono-, di-, and tri-substituted phenolamides produced through synthetic microbial consortia of polyamine and aromatic yeast strains

A plant-derived monoterpene indole alkaloid (MIA) produced from tabersonine by metabolically engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Through CRISPR/Cas9-mediated multiplex genome integration and multiple metabolic engineering strategies, the final production titer reached ~16.5 mg/L, representing more than 3,800,000-fold improvement over the parent strain. Vindoline serves as the key biosynthetic precursor to the anticancer drugs vinblastine and vincristine.

A rhamnosylated protopanaxatriol-type ginsenoside produced de novo from glucose by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Achieved a maximum yield of 1.3 g/L through fed-batch fermentation. Exhibits anti-inflammatory, neuroprotective, and metabolic disease amelioration activities.

A rhamnosylated protopanaxatriol-type ginsenoside produced de novo from glucose by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Achieved a maximum yield of 3.6 g/L through fed-batch fermentation. Demonstrates cardioprotective, anti-ischemic, and anti-arrhythmic effects.
A stilbenoid compound produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
A methylated stilbenoid produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

Nanoscale cellulose fibers (also called nanocelluloses, microfibrillated cellulose, or cellulose nanocrystals) produced as a co-product during fermentation of Kraft pulp residues. The consolidated bioprocessing increases cellulosic content from 78% to 87% and achieves nanofibrillation without high-energy mechanical processing, representing a multibillion-dollar industry potential for applications in food, hygiene, medical, cosmetic, and pharmaceutical products.

fields.grna_map: LAC12 integrated at chromosome XVI (CS8); LAC4 integrated at chromosome VII (CS6) using CRISPR-Cas9
fields.description: A novel platform chemical and anhydrohexitol sugar alcohol produced from red macroalgal agarose via metabolically engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. The engineered strain D452-L124 expresses heterologous lactose permease (LAC12) and β-galactosidase (LAC4) from Kluyveromyces lactis for agarobiose uptake and hydrolysis, with endogenous aldose reductase (GRE3) converting AHG to AHGol. Achieved 41.18 g/L titer with 0.47 g/g yield in fed-batch fermentation. Can be chemically converted to isosorbide for bioplastic production.

Sesquiterpene precursor to (+)-nootkatone with citrus fruit aroma, produced via metabolic engineering of Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing (+)-valencene synthase CnVS combined with MVA pathway optimization.

A hydroxylated sesquiterpenoid product of (+)-valencene oxidation, serving as an intermediate to (+)-nootkatone. Produced through whole-cell biotransformation using engineered S. cerevisiae expressing HPO, AtCPR, and ADH1. Achieved 327.8 mg/L in resting cell assays (PK2-35) and 95.7 mg/L in cell culture (PK2-25).

Ethylene (ethene) production in genetically engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via two metabolic routes: plant pathway (using ACC synthase and ACC oxidase with S-adenosylmethionine) and microbial pathway (using ethylene-forming enzyme from Pseudomonas syringae with 2-oxoglutarate and arginine). Flux balance analysis predicted maximal yields of 7-8 mol ethylene/10 mol glucose. The microbial system showed superior performance with experimental production rates of 147-210 mg/g dry weight/h. Optimization strategies include using glutamate as nitrogen source, modifying glutamate dehydrogenase cofactor specificity from NADP to NAD, and proline supplementation.

Key intermediate in benzylisoquinoline alkaloid biosynthesis, produced from dopamine using crude enzymes from transgenic Escherichia coli. Final yield of 55 mg/liter within 1 hour. Nonnarcotic alkaloid of pharmaceutical significance for antimalarial and anticancer drug development.

Aporphine alkaloid produced from dopamine via reticuline using co-cultures of transgenic Escherichia coli and Saccharomyces cerevisiae cells. Final yield of 7.2 mg/liter culture medium within 72 hours. Has medicinal properties including protecting HDL during oxidant stress, preventing atherosclerotic disease, and inhibiting HIV-1 cell-killing.
β-amyrin hydroxylated at C-16α position by CYP716Y1 from Bupleurum falcatum, produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae
α-amyrin hydroxylated at C-16α position by CYP716Y1 from Bupleurum falcatum, produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Monoglucosylated saponin produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing UGT73C11 glucosyltransferase

The central biosynthetic intermediate for all monoterpene indole alkaloids, produced de novo in Saccharomyces cerevisiae through metabolic engineering of 21 plant-derived genes and 3 gene deletions, reaching titers of ~0.5 mg/L

Nystose (GF3) produced via transfructosylation activity of invertases from Saccharomyces cerevisiae CAT-1 and Rhodotorula mucilaginosa at high sucrose concentrations (>10%), reaching 13.3 g/L (S. cerevisiae) and 12.6 g/L (R. mucilaginosa) with 20% sucrose.

Platform chemical and antiseptic additive produced de novo in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via heterologous expression of 6-methylsalicylic acid synthase (MSAS) and 6-MSA decarboxylase. Exhibits antimicrobial, antifungal, and protein stabilizing properties. Used as preservative in pharmaceuticals and as precursor for menthol and vitamin synthesis.

Produced by in vivo methylation of 3-methylphenol using orcinol-O-methyltransferase (OOMT2) from Chinese rose hybrids. Less toxic and more volatile than 3-MP, extracted using dodecane. Valuable precursor for high-priced flavor compound vanillin.
Mentioned as one of the products S. cerevisiae is utilized for in industrial biotechnology, though no specific details are provided in this paper.

An important intermediate in the shikonin biosynthesis pathway. Produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through heterologous expression of p-hydroxybenzoate:geranyltransferase (PGT) from Arnebia euchroma. Production was optimized by engineering both the mevalonate pathway (overexpressing tHMG1) and shikimic pathway (overexpressing codon-optimized UbiC), achieving a titer of 179.29 mg/L in shake flask fermentation.

fields.grna_map: pRCC-N zwf1 gRNA (for CRISPR/Cas9-mediated ZWF1 locus modification)
fields.description: Produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via the shikimate pathway from glucose. Achieved a titer of 1244 mg L-1 with a yield of 31 mg g-1 glucose, representing the highest yield reported in yeast. Key precursor for adipic acid used in nylon, plastics, lubricants and softeners.

fields.grna_map: pRCC-N zwf1 gRNA (for CRISPR/Cas9-mediated ZWF1 locus modification)
fields.description: First-time production demonstrated in Saccharomyces cerevisiae, reaching 682 mg L-1 from glucose. Phenolic compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammative, antimutagenic and anticancer properties. Applications in pharmaceutical and chemical industries.

Gaseous metabolic product of engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae central carbon metabolism. Production rates range from 15.4-34.4 mmol g-1 h-1. Ratio of CO2 to ethanol increases more than 50% with decreasing glucose uptake rates, indicating increased TCA cycle flux.

β-fructofuranosidase enzyme produced both extracellularly and intracellularly by a thermotolerant mutant of Saccharomyces cerevisiae during molasses fermentation at 40°C. The mutant exhibited 1.8-fold higher extracellular (1839 IU l⁻¹ h⁻¹) and 2.6-fold higher intracellular (8575 IU l⁻¹ h⁻¹) productivity compared to the wild parent.
Organic acid present in wine after fermentation of high-sugar must, with strain-dependent concentrations of 0.41–0.76 g/L affecting wine acidity profile
Higher alcohols (fusel alcohols) produced as byproducts during fermentation of blackstrap molasses by Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Yield minimized to 0.23 g per 100 L under optimized conditions.
Aldehyde byproducts formed during fermentation, measured by potassium permanganate test time (PTT). Minimized to 14.6 minutes under optimized conditions.

Volatile phenol contributing to 'horse, stable, leather or medicinal' off-flavours in wine. Produced by Dekkera bruxellensis from 4-vinylphenol. Highest levels (720 μg/l) observed in mixed fermentations with Pichia guilliermondii and D. bruxellensis.

Volatile phenol contributing to wine off-flavours. Produced from ferulic acid via 4-vinylguaiacol. Highest levels (545 μg/l) observed in mixed fermentations with Pichia guilliermondii and D. bruxellensis.
Vinylphenol produced by Pichia guilliermondii from p-coumaric acid via decarboxylation. Precursor to 4-ethylphenol. Levels exceeding 4500 μg/l observed in fermentations with P. guilliermondii.
Vinylphenol produced from ferulic acid. Precursor to 4-ethylguaiacol. Detected in wines fermented with various yeast combinations including Pichia guilliermondii and Dekkera bruxellensis.

A triterpene compound (C30 isoprenoid) produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae by heterologous expression of b-amyrin synthase from Artemisia annua. Production levels of 6 mg/L were achieved through metabolic engineering of the yeast sterol pathway. Serves as a precursor to various bioactive triterpene saponins with pharmaceutical and nutraceutical applications.

Dihydroxyacetone (DHA) accumulates in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains with disrupted DAK1/DAK2 genes and overexpressed GCY1 under micro-aerobic conditions. It is an intermediate metabolite in the glycerol dissimilation pathway.

A monoterpene with a pleasant floral scent, produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through fusion expression of farnesyl diphosphate synthase and linalool synthase. The highest reported titre was 240.64 ± 5.31 µg l⁻¹ in a 3-l fermenter.
Terpenoid aroma compound with high odor activity value (>100). Strain-specific production detected only in genotypes I, II, X, and XII across both fermentation modes.

A nonribosomally synthesized polypeptide (multi-L-arginyl-poly(L-aspartic acid)) produced in transgenic Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing cyanophycin synthetase from Synechocystis sp. PCC 6308. The polymer exists in water-soluble (major) and water-insoluble (minor) forms, with maximum accumulation of 6.9% (wt/wt) in cells. Composed primarily of aspartic acid and arginine, with minor lysine incorporation.
Aromatic precursor in the engineered pathway, produced from 3-dehydroshikimate by heterologous 3-dehydroshikimate dehydratase.

An aromatic intermediate in the heterologous muconic acid pathway, produced by decarboxylation of protocatechuic acid by the bacterial enzyme AroY-Ciso in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Represents a key bottleneck step in muconic acid production, with titers reaching up to 88 mg/L in optimized strains.

Glucosylated form of vanillin produced by co-expressing Arabidopsis thaliana UDP-glycosyltransferase UGT72E2 in vanillin-producing S. pombe. This form is less toxic to yeast, more water-soluble, and enables accumulation of higher yields. 56% of total vanillin potential was converted to the glucoside.

Enantiopure vicinal diol produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain DP-BDH1 overexpressing butanediol dehydrogenase (Bdh1p) and formate dehydrogenase (Fdh1p). Used as antifreeze agent (freezing point -60°C) and platform chemical for synthesis of methyl ethyl ketone, acetoin, diacetyl, and polymer precursors.

Enantiopure vicinal diol produced from 2,3-pentanedione using purified Bdh1p or engineered yeast cell extracts. Serves as a chiral building block for asymmetric synthesis of compounds with two stereogenic centers.
Enantiopure vicinal diol produced from 2,3-hexanedione using purified Bdh1p or engineered yeast cell extracts. Serves as a chiral building block for asymmetric synthesis.
Enantiopure vicinal diol produced from 3,4-hexanedione using purified Bdh1p or engineered yeast cell extracts. Serves as a chiral building block for asymmetric synthesis.
A flavone produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing flavone synthase I (FSI) from parsley, derived from cinnamic acid precursor. Detected only in strains expressing FSI.

A flavone produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via two distinct flavone synthases (soluble FSI and membrane-bound FSII), derived from p-coumaric acid precursor. Acts as a feedback inhibitor of flavanone biosynthesis.
A flavone produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae, derived from caffeic acid precursor. Does not exhibit feedback inhibition of flavanone biosynthesis.

An unhydroxylated flavanone produced by recombinant Saccharomyces cerevisiae from cinnamic acid, achieving a titer of 16.3 mg/liter, which is 22 times higher than previously reported recombinant E. coli strains.
A hydroxylated flavanone produced by recombinant Saccharomyces cerevisiae from caffeic acid, achieving a titer of 6.5 mg/liter. This represents the first microbial biosynthesis of eriodictyol.

Collective mixture of higher alcohols (n-propanol, isobutanol, isoamyl alcohol, and 2-phenyl ethanol) produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains. Total concentration is critical for Baijiu (Chinese liquor) quality, with optimal levels providing unique aroma and mellow taste while excessive amounts cause off-flavors.

Sesquiterpene produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains via tandem heterozygous deletions of ubc7, ssm4/doa10, and pho86 genes, achieving 125 mg/L yields in shake flasks (11-fold increase over base strain).

Recombinant human BiP (GRP78) protein produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae, secreted into the culture medium, correctly processed, biologically active with high ATPase activity. Potential therapeutic applications in cancer, rheumatoid arthritis, and neurodegenerative disorders.

A biopolymer produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae using cytosolic acetyl-CoA as precursor. Optimal production achieved with mixed glucose and ethanol carbon sources, yielding highest volumetric productivity of 8.23 mg/L·h⁻¹ at dilution rate 0.1 h⁻¹.
Enzymes (e.g., filter paper activity/FPA) secreted by Fusarium oxysporum for cellulose degradation. Tolerant to ethanol and suitable for consolidated bioprocessing.

An important building-block petrochemical and monomer for plastics production, engineered in Saccharomyces cerevisiae via a pathway using phenylalanine as precursor, with heterologous expression of phenylalanine ammonia lyase (PAL2) from Arabidopsis thaliana and native phenylacrylate decarboxylase (FDC1).
Produced from tyrosine via tyrosine ammonia lyase (TAL) as a test case for heterologous polyketide pathways.

Heterologously produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via pathway engineering using four genes (PAL/TAL, C4H, 4CL, BAS) and synthetic enzyme fusions. Achieved 7.5 mg/L with precursor feeding and 2.8 mg/L de novo synthesis. Sensorially relevant quantities produced in wine fermentations (3.5 mg/L aerobic, 0.68 mg/L anaerobic).

Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain overexpressing ABZ1 and ABZ2 genes from wine yeast strains, with deletions in TRP3 and ARO7 and a feedback-resistant ARO4 gene, produces pABA from glycerol and ethanol carbon sources. Achieved titers of 1.57 mM (215 mg/L) in fed-batch bioreactor experiments.

A natural carotenoid dicarboxylic acid with high pharmaceutical values for cardiovascular, neuropsychological diseases, and cancer therapy. Produced from glucose by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through systematic optimization including precursor engineering (CIT2 knockout), forward enzyme fusion (PsCrtZ-CsCCD2), and medium/fermentation optimization, achieving a titer of 12.43 ± 0.62 mg/L in 5-L bioreactor.

Produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing cyanobacterial aldehyde decarbonylases (ADOs) from Synechococcus elongatus and Crocosphaera watsonii. These hydrocarbons are generated via conversion of fatty aldehydes and serve as biofuels.
CoA-activated form of angelic acid produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Serves as a precursor for pharmacologically active angelates with anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties.

A monoterpene ester with a pleasant scent, widely used in fragrance and cosmetic industries, and possessing antinociceptive activity. Produced via engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing geraniol synthase (GES) and alcohol acyltransferase (SAAT), with metabolic enhancements including Erg20 mutants, tHMG1, IDI1, and MAF1 overexpression.

fields.grna_map: gRNA used for genomic integrations (CAN1) and knockouts (EXG1, SPR1, TSC13)
fields.description: Anthocyanin pigment produced de novo in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae from glucose, intended as a food colorant and health-promoting compound.
Apigenin-6C-glucoside produced via engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae using either indirect (F2H + CGT) or direct (GtUF6CGT1) glycosylation routes.
Apigenin-8C-glucoside produced via engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae using indirect glycosylation (F2H + CGT) routes.
Luteolin-6C-glucoside produced via engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae using either indirect (F2H + CGT) or direct (GtUF6CGT1) glycosylation routes.
Luteolin-8C-glucoside produced via engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae using indirect glycosylation (F2H + CGT) routes.

A diterpene resin acid produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via metabolic engineering and protein engineering. Used as an industrial intermediate for coatings, printing inks, plasticizers, and adhesives.

Diunsaturated wax esters (C38-WE to C44-WE) produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae by expressing heterologous enzymes including a wax synthase from Simmondsia chinensis. Used in cosmetics, pharmaceuticals, and lubricants.
Compounds such as C22:1-FOH produced in engineered yeast using a fatty acyl reductase from Marinobacter aquaeolei. Used in specialty chemicals and cosmetics.

A diterpenoid precursor to forskolin, produced via metabolic engineering of Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Engineered through overexpression of rate-limiting genes (tHMG1, ERG20), fusion proteins (Bts1p-Erg20F96Cp), and truncation of diterpene synthases (tCfTPS2, tCfTPS3). Achieved a titer of 3 g/L in fed-batch fermentation.

Cytosolic production in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains via heterologous expression of alsLpᵒᵖ (Lactobacillus plantarum), aldcLlᵒᵖ (Lactococcus lactis), and BDH1 (S. cerevisiae) genes. ATG32 knockout increased titer 23.6-fold in test-tube cultures.

Natural antioxidant produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae by heterologous expression of two genes (Gfegt1 and Gfegt2) from Grifola frondosa. Production reached 20.61 mg/L after optimization of fermentation conditions.
Isomer of taxadiene (taxa-4(20),11(12)-diene) generated as a minor product during engineered taxadiene production.

A phenolic glycoside used as a neuroprotective medicine, with applications in treating nervous system diseases such as ischemic stroke, epilepsy, and dementia. Produced de novo from glucose via engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
Precursor of the anti-obesity agent celastrol, produced via engineered yeast strains expressing friedelin synthase (KdFRS) and cytochrome P450 enzymes (CYP712K1).
A platform chemical used in the production of dyes, perfumes, and pharmaceutical compounds. Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae achieved a titer of 567.9 mg·l⁻¹ in SCD medium.
A grape flavor compound produced by extending the anthranilate pathway with anthranilic acid methyltransferase 1 (MtAAMT1). Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae achieved 414 mg·l⁻¹ in YPD medium.
Branched-chain biodiesel produced by engineered yeast using endogenous fatty acids and isobutanol, exhibiting improved cold flow properties.
Branched-chain biodiesel synthesized by engineered S. cerevisiae using endogenous fatty acids and isoamyl alcohol via wax ester synthases.
Branched-chain biodiesel produced by engineered yeast using endogenous fatty acids and active amyl alcohol, derived from overlapping isobutanol pathways.

Key intermediate for corticosteroid production, produced via biotransformation of progesterone using recombinant Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing fungal steroid hydroxylases from Aspergillus ochraceus or Rhizopus oryzae.
Tricyclic sesquiterpene produced in engineered Escherichia coli as a high-density jet fuel precursor. Achieved titers of 727.9 mg/L using heterologous MVA pathway and promoter engineering.
Tricyclic sesquiterpene jet fuel precursor produced in engineered Escherichia coli (780.3 mg/L) and Saccharomyces cerevisiae (344 mg/L) using heterologous pathways and promoter optimization.
Tricyclic sesquiterpene synthesized in engineered Escherichia coli (77.5 mg/L) as a novel jet fuel feedstock using codon-optimized synthases and dynamic metabolic engineering.

Prebiotic oligosaccharide produced via controlled enzymatic hydrolysis of pretreated outer tunic biomass using cellulases (e.g., endoglucanase EG5 and cellobiohydrolase CBHI), achieving up to 49.7 wt.% conversion yield. Functions as a prebiotic for gut microbiota.

Alcoholic beverage produced by distilling fermented agave juice (Agave tequilana Weber var. azul) using Saccharomyces cerevisiae. The study improved productivity through continuous fermentation with yeast extract supplementation and aeration.
Active pharmaceutical ingredients produced using Saccharomyces cerevisiae as a biocatalyst, mentioned as part of the reactor's potential applications.
A diterpene precursor for the anti-cancer drug fusicoccin A, produced by genetically modified Saccharomyces cerevisiae using fed-batch fermentation strategies to increase yield
Polymer formed from D-lactic acid monomers; improves thermostability of poly L-lactic acid through stereo-complex formation.
Saccharomyces cerevisiae biomass enriched with copper ions through cultivation in media supplemented with copper sulfate, intended as a dietary supplement for humans and animals.

Produced as intermediate at 260.1 mg/L in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Methyl ethyl ketone (MEK) formed from meso-2,3-butanediol via B12-dependent diol dehydratase, serving as precursor for 2-butanol synthesis.

Sesquiterpene lactone precursor for antimalarial drug artemisinin. Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae produced 1.70 g/L in 5 L bioreactor fermentation with DHAA/AA ratio of 9.84. Direct bioproduction eliminates chemical hydrogenation step required for artemisinic acid precursor route.

Recombinant SESTC cellulase (approximately 44 kDa) produced by CRISPR-Cas9 engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae. The enzyme exhibits endo-1,4-β-glucanase, exo-1,4-β-glucanase, and β-xylanase activities for hydrolysis of orange peel cellulose, with activities 17-35 fold higher than wild-type yeast.

Short-chain ester with fruity aroma produced by engineered Clostridium beijerinckii through in vivo alcoholysis activity of heterologously expressed Wickerhamomyces anomalus Eat1 enzyme. The process upgrades ethyl butyrate using endogenously produced butanol, achieving a maximum titer of 0.29 mM.

An important aroma compound in Baijiu with nutty aroma and key flavor compound. Has beneficial effects on cardiovascular and cerebrovascular health. Production was increased up to 10.55 mg/L in engineered haploid S. cerevisiae strains and 9.47 mg/L in diploid strains through metabolic engineering of BDH1 and BDH2 genes.

Primary tryptophan derivative produced by Saccharomyces cerevisiae EC1118 through the kynurenine pathway. Highest concentration achieved was 9.146 ± 0.585 mg/L in YNBT400 medium (400 mg/L TRP). Neuroprotective and antioxidant compound with antagonist activity against nicotinic cholinergic receptor α7 and NMDA receptors. Potential therapeutic applications in neurological disorders.

Key intermediate metabolite in the kynurenine pathway produced by Saccharomyces cerevisiae EC1118 from tryptophan. Acts as a ligand for the aryl hydrocarbon receptor, regulating gene expression and immune function. Accumulated up to 0.104 ± 0.046 mg/L in YNBT100 medium.

Downstream metabolite in the kynurenine pathway produced by Saccharomyces cerevisiae EC1118. Reached concentrations of 0.101 ± 0.013 mg/L in YNBT400 medium and showed peak accumulation during stationary phase.
Metabolite in the kynurenine pathway produced by Saccharcharomyces cerevisiae EC1118. Shows relatively stable extracellular concentrations during fermentation with slight variations.

5-hydroxytryptamine produced by Saccharomyces cerevisiae EC1118 through the melatonin biosynthesis pathway. Detected in fermentation experiments, showing stable production after initial accumulation. Only intermediate of the melatonin pathway consistently observed.

Two structural isomers of melatonin produced by Saccharomyces cerevisiae EC1118. Successfully measured in YNBT400 fermentation and whole-cell biotransformation experiments. Exhibit antioxidant and cytoprotective activities depending on side chain positions. Production varies with growth phase, reaching maximum at 72-144 h.

Produced by Saccharomyces cerevisiae EC1118 through decarboxylation of tryptophan. Detected in whole-cell biotransformation experiments, reaching 0.066 ± 0.001 mg/L after 24 h. Key intermediate in the alternative melatonin biosynthesis pathway.

A nondairy probiotic beverage produced by fermenting mango slurry with Lactobacillus plantarum and Saccharomyces cerevisiae DV10. The fermentation yields high levels of lactic acid (up to 6.12 g/L), increases total phenolic content, and produces volatile compounds including alcohols and esters. The final product exhibits strong antioxidant activity (ABTS radical scavenging, ferric-reducing antioxidant power) and LDL oxidation inhibition.

α,ω-dicarboxylic acid (DCA) produced via whole-cell biotransformation of lauric acid from coconut milk factory wastewater using engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae BCM expressing CYP52A17SS cytochrome P450 enzyme. Serves as a versatile building block for polymers, cosmetic intermediates, and pharmaceuticals. Maximum yield of approximately 38 µg/L obtained in YPCCW medium at 30°C and pH 5-5.5 after 120 h.

ω-hydroxy fatty acid intermediate produced via ω-oxidation of lauric acid from coconut milk factory wastewater using engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae BCM expressing CYP52A17SS cytochrome P450 enzyme. Acts as a precursor to 1,12-dodecanedioic acid. Maximum yield of approximately 60 µg/L obtained in YPCCW medium at 30°C and pH 5-5.5 after 96 h.

A structurally complex labdane-type diterpenoid pharmaceutical produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae from glucose. Used as a cyclic AMP booster for treatment of glaucoma and heart failure. The engineered yeast strain achieved titers of 40 mg/L in fermenter cultures.
Stereoselective formation achieved through specific diTPS combinations.
Diastereomer formed through engineered diTPS combinations, demonstrating stereochemical control.
Produced at 310 mg/L in engineered S. cerevisiae, tripling previous titers.
Formed from syn-CPP by SsSCS, demonstrating product plasticity.
Novel diterpene scaffold not previously reported in nature.
Novel diterpene scaffold not previously reported in nature.
Fusion protein displayed on yeast cell surface for visualization and catalysis. Successfully converted linoleic acid (LA) to trans-10, cis-12 CLA with retained enzymatic activity.
The level of (S)-3-methyl-1-pentanol increased from 6.5 mg/L to 793.5 mg/L by engineering the selectivity of KIVD and relieving the steric hindrance of LeuA.

fields.product_name: Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae for xylan catabolism
fields.description: Engineered S. cerevisiae expressing xylanase 1 (XNA1), xylosidase 2 (XD2), and arabinofuranosidase (ABF) from Ustilago bevomyces, enabling growth and ethanol production (up to 0.22 g/L) on xylan as a sole carbon source in minimal medium.
Adaptive evolution of the engineered strain improved xylan utilization, resulting in a 50% increase in cell growth and 23% higher ethanol production (up to 0.32 g/L in complex medium).
Improved AAA production phenotypes as a surrogate to improve muconic acid production, with up to 2-fold higher total AAA production.
Engineered yeast strain capable of fermenting both xylose and glucose to ethanol. Achieved high ethanol yields and maintained good cell viability at optimal conditions.
Engineered bacterium capable of fermenting both xylose and glucose to ethanol. Demonstrated high ethanol yields at 30°C but faced viability issues at 37°C.
Engineered yeast strain used for co-fermentation of glucose and xylose to produce ethanol.
Ethanol-tolerant strain with mutations K201N, G216S, and N225Stop in SPT15.
Ethanol-tolerant strain with mutations L76V and L175S in SPT15, producing approximately 25% more ethanol than control.
Ethanol-tolerant strain with mutations S42N, C78R, S163P, and I212N in SPT15, producing approximately 25% more ethanol than control.
Ethanol-tolerant strain with mutations F10S and M197K in SPT15.
Ethanol-tolerant strain with mutations K15T, W26C, and G192D in SPT15.
Genome-integrated strain with SPT15-M2 allele, showing enhanced ethanol tolerance and increased ethanol yield (0.39 g/g) and productivity (3.2 g/L/h).
Genome-integrated strain with SPT15-M3 allele, showing enhanced ethanol tolerance and increased ethanol yield (0.39 g/g) and productivity (3.2 g/L/h).
Under nitrogen limiting conditions, major products shifted to medium chain length FAEEs (C10 and C12).
Addition of pentadecanoic acid led to production of 3.6±0.6 mg/L C15-FAEE, accounting for 22% of total FAEEs.
Endogenous production of C16 and C18 FAEEs was observed, with increased levels under fatty acid doping conditions.

Fatty acid ethyl esters (FAEEs) produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae from glycerol using endogenously produced ethanol. The engineered YPH499 strain produced 0.24 g/L FAEEs, while the enhanced fps1Δ gpd2Δ strain produced 0.52 g/L FAEEs, representing a 2.1-fold improvement. This microbial system enables biodiesel production from low-cost glycerol feedstock.

FAEEs are biodiesel substitutes produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through the expression of a heterologous wax ester synthase gene (ws2). The production was enhanced by chromosomal integration and overexpression of endogenous acyl-CoA binding protein (ACB1) and a bacterial NADP+-dependent glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase (gapN).

Muconic acid is a dehydroshikimate-derived compound. Previous studies have shown its production in E. coli and S. cerevisiae, with strategies including overexpression of transketolase gene tkl1 to increase the flux of the precursor erythrose-4-phosphate (E4P).
A 15-carbon isoprenoid (sesquiterpene) and precursor of the antimalarial drug artemisinin.

A ganoderic acid (GA) with antitumor and other significant pharmacological activities, biosynthesized in Saccharomyces cerevisiae by expressing a cytochrome P450 gene (cyp5150l8) from Ganoderma lucidum.
Enhanced production of isobutanol from xylose using engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae with a mitochondrial-compartmentalized isobutanol production pathway.
A triterpenoid produced by BfOSC2 in Saccharomyces cerevisiae, serving as a precursor for morolic acid biosynthesis.
KDX is a key intermediate in the BT biosynthetic pathway, whose reduction is crucial for improving BT yield.

Furfuryl alcohol is produced through the whole-cell biotransformation of furfural (FAL) by Saccharomyces cerevisiae NL22. The process achieves high conversion (98%) and selectivity (87.9%) under optimized conditions (30 °C, 62 mM FAL, 1.3 mol glucose/mol FAL within 8 h). A fed-batch strategy yielded 122 mM FOL within 24 h.

An important aromatic compound used in perfumes, cosmetics, pharmaceuticals, foods, and beverages. Produced via the Ehrlich pathway using L-phenylalanine (L-Phe) as a precursor by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

DDGS is a by-product of bioethanol production, rich in protein, fiber, and lipids. It is used as a high-quality animal feed component, with protein content ranging from 308.5 to 360.8 g kg−1 DM and good digestibility.

Glutathione is a non-protein thiol compound with important physiological functions such as bioreduction, protection against oxidative stress, detoxification of xenobiotics, and enzymatic activity. It is used in the treatment of diseases like liver cirrhosis, HIV, cancer, and Alzheimer's, as well as in food additives and cosmetics.

Recombinant outer-surface protein A with an N-terminally truncated form from the Lyme disease spirochete Borrelia burgdorferi, expressed in Saccharomyces cerevisiae at high production levels for potential use as a vaccine candidate.

Engineered industrial strains of Saccharomyces cerevisiae (e.g., TMB 3400, TMB 3006, 424A(LNF-ST)) with heterologous xylose utilization pathways (xylose reductase and xylitol dehydrogenase) for improved ethanol production from lignocellulosic hydrolysates.
Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains with bacterial or fungal arabinose utilization pathways for co-fermentation of arabinose and xylose in lignocellulosic hydrolysates.
Reduction of furfural to furfuryl alcohol by overexpression of NADH-dependent ADH1 (m6ADH1) or wild-type ADH1 in Saccharomyces cerevisiae, mitigating inhibitory effects on xylose fermentation.
Ethyl esters (e.g., ethyl octanoate, ethyl nonanoate, ethyl decanoate) were produced in higher amounts at 20°C compared to 30°C, with levels up to 2.13 mg/L.
Acetate esters (e.g., ethyl acetate, isoamyl acetate) were produced in higher amounts at 20°C compared to 30°C, with levels up to 4.61 mg/L.
A tetraterpenoid precursor of vitamin A used in food and feed products, produced by recombinant Saccharomyces cerevisiae under H2O2 stress.
A flavonoid glycoside produced alongside N7O by the same engineered yeast system. The production ratio between N7O and N4O can be controlled by altering the glucose content in the culture medium.

A novel glucoside of vanillyl alcohol synthesized using maltase from Saccharomyces cerevisiae. It exhibits good antioxidant activity and stability in the gastrointestinal tract, hydrolyzing on the brush border membrane of enterocytes to release vanillyl alcohol and glucose.
Aromatic compound used as a major feedstock for production of liquid crystal polymers, particularly for the poly-condensate VectranTM, which is used in space technology applications.
Enzymatic hydrolysis of NMMO-pretreated pinewood and elmwood yields total reducing sugars, which are precursors for ethanol production.

Biomass production of non-Saccharomyces wine yeasts (Lachancea thermotolerans, Issatchenkia orientalis, Metschnikowia pulcherrima) and Saccharomyces cerevisiae for use as starter cultures in wine fermentation, influencing organoleptic properties.
Iso-butanol is noted as a potential gasoline additive with superior energy content and lower volatility compared to ethanol, though the study's main focus is on 1-butanol.
An endo-β-mannanase from Aspergillus aculeatus, expressed in Saccharomyces cerevisiae for hydrolysis of galactomannan.
An α-galactosidase from Talaromyces emersonii, expressed in Saccharomyces cerevisiae for removal of galactose side chains from mannan.
A β-mannosidase from Aspergillus niger, expressed in A. niger for hydrolysis of oligomannans to release mannose units.
A synthetic β-mannosidase from Cellvibrio mixtus, codon-optimized for expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae and expressed in A. niger for hydrolysis of oligomannans.
Includes C10:0, C12:0, and C14:0 fatty acids, produced in engineered yeast strains.
Levansucrase from Rahnella aquatilis, hyper-secreted by Saccharomyces cerevisiae for the enzymatic production of levan.
Produced in Escherichia coli by expressing an α-dioxygenase from Oryza sativa, along with other enzymes, to tailor carbon number during fatty aldehyde formation. Achieved titers up to 1.95 g/L.
Produced in Rhodosporidium toruloides by expressing a fatty acyl-CoA reductase from Marinobacter aquaeolei VT8. Achieved titers up to 8 g/L in a 7L bioreactor.
Produced in Escherichia coli by expressing specific thioesterases and fatty acyl-CoA reductases. Achieved titers up to 1.65 g/L with a yield of 0.13 g/g glucose.
Produced by Clostridium acetobutylicum CH02 from a mixture of glucose and xylose. The solvents yield was improved by mixed fermentation with Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
Reducing sugars produced from water hyacinth biomass via enzymatic hydrolysis using cellulases and beta-glucosidases.
Xylobiose obtained as a major product during saccharification of alkali treated rice straw with enzymes from Malbranchea cinnamomea and Thermomyces lanuginosus, known for functional food properties.
Xylo-oligosaccharides produced during hydrolysis of pretreated substrates, particularly from alkali treated rice straw with enzymes from M. cinnamomea.

A versatile chemical used as an anti-freeze agent, solvent, and precursor for other chemicals like methylethylketone (MEK) and 1,3-butadiene. Produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing bacterial 2,3-BD biosynthetic pathway enzymes (acetolactate synthase, acetolactate decarboxylase, and butanediol dehydrogenase).
Fermentation of the non-detoxified hydrolyzate obtained after combined pretreatment and hydrolysis of rice straw by Comamonas sp. yielded 35.86% of PHB without any nutrient supplementation.

Protopanaxadiol (PPD) is an active compound in Panax ginseng, used as an antineoplastic and antidepressant candidate medicine. It is produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through a de novo synthesis pathway involving a cytochrome P450-type PPD synthase (PPDS, CYP716A47) and its reductase CPR (cytochrome P450 reductase).
Ethanol produced from softwood hemicellulosic hydrolysate using Saccharomyces cerevisiae yeast, achieving a yield of 74.27% of the theoretical maximum.
Saccharomyces cerevisiae Ethanol Red® was genetically engineered to produce extracellular β-galactosidase, enabling lactose hydrolysis in cheese whey for integrated bioethanol production.

Ethanol produced from the liquid fraction of sorghum bagasse after nanofiltration membrane separation to remove butanol, followed by fermentation using a transgenic Saccharomyces cerevisiae capable of utilizing xylose.

A plant-derived pentacyclic triterpenoid compound with various biological activities, including anti-inflammation, anti-viral, anti-tumor, and hepatoprotective effects. Produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae by optimizing the pairing efficiency between cytochrome P450 monooxygenase and reductase, reconstructing the galactose regulatory network, and enhancing precursor supply.

Produced via catalytic dehydration of 2,3-BDO. The process was evaluated for financial viability and environmental benefits through techno-economic analysis (TEA) and life cycle assessment (LCA). The minimum product selling price (MPSP) was estimated to be $1.90/kg, with a 100-year global warming potential (GWP100) of 0.37 kg CO2 eq/kg and fossil energy consumption (FEC) of 3.1 MJ/kg.
Reprogrammed S. cerevisiae strain that replaces alcoholic fermentation with lipogenesis as the main pathway for metabolizing glucose, producing FFAs at high titers.
An intermediate in the Q6 biosynthetic pathway derived from pABA, accumulating in Yah1p/Arh1p-depleted yeast strains. It results from the prenylation of pABA by Coq2p and subsequent decarboxylation.
An intermediate in the Q6 biosynthetic pathway derived from 4-HB, accumulating in Yah1p/Arh1p-depleted yeast strains. It results from the prenylation of 4-HB by Coq2p and subsequent decarboxylation.
A product of the prenylation of pABA by Coq2p, which can be further converted into Q6 via an NH2-to-OH conversion step.
A product of the prenylation of 4-HB by Coq2p, serving as a key intermediate in the Q6 biosynthetic pathway.
An intermediate in Q6 biosynthesis, the substrate of the mono-oxygenase Coq7p, which hydroxylates it to form Q6.

GSH is a non-protein thiol compound with antioxidant properties, used in medicine, food additives, and cosmetics. Produced via fermentation using Saccharomyces cerevisiae T65 with RQ feedback control to optimize yield and content.
Sugars extracted from carob waste using whey as a solvent, containing sucrose, glucose, and fructose.
Medium derived from cheese whey, containing lactose as the primary sugar, used for fermentation by Kluyveromyces lactis.
An intermediate in the glucaric acid biosynthesis pathway, produced from myo-inositol by myo-inositol oxygenase (Miox).
Pyrazines, pyrroles, and pyridines contributing to nutty and roasted aromas in roasted coffee, elevated by S. cerevisiae fermentation.

Beer produced using mixed fermentations of Saccharomyces cerevisiae and Torulaspora delbrueckii, resulting in enhanced aroma profiles including higher levels of higher alcohols (except β-phenyl ethanol), ethyl acetate, and isoamyl acetate.
Maltose is another fermentable sugar produced by Bacillus during starch hydrolysis. It is utilized by some yeasts for growth.
Nerolidol is a FOH isomer that accumulates intracellularly under acidic conditions. It is produced as a byproduct during the cultivation of Saccharomyces cerevisiae ATCC 64031.
An aromatic alcohol with a rose-like fragrance, used in the production of perfume and other cosmetics.

GABA is a major inhibitory neurotransmitter with physiological functions including antihypertensive, diuretic, and relaxation properties. Produced by Saccharomyces cerevisiae SC125 and Lactobacillus plantarum BC114 coculture in mulberry beverage fermentation.

fields.product_name: Fruity esters (e.g., ethyl caproate, ethyl propionate, butyl acetate, ethyl butyrate, ethyl caprylate, ethyl caprate)
fields.description: Produced during coculture fermentation, contributing to the fruity and floral aroma of the mulberry beverage.
An alcohol produced during fermentation, contributing to the rose and honey flavor of the beverage.
A medium branched-chain ester used as a biofuel and industrial chemical with applications in coatings, adhesives, printing inks, fragrances, and cosmetics.
A medium branched-chain ester with banana flavor, used as flavoring additives in foods and drinks, and as a green solvent in surface coatings and pharmaceuticals.

Important aromatic substances in alcoholic beverages, including ethyl hexanoate (EH), ethyl octanoate (EO), and ethyl decanoate (ED). Enhanced production was achieved through metabolic engineering of Saccharomyces cerevisiae CA strain by overexpressing genes involved in acetyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA biosynthesis pathways, fatty acid synthase genes (FAS1, FAS2), and introducing strawberry alcohol acyltransferase (SAAT).
Aroma compound with anise seed aroma, produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae CA strain. Production increased 25.89-fold to 7.53 mg/L through metabolic engineering.
Aroma compound with sour apple aroma, produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae CA strain. Production increased 7.27-fold to 13.65 mg/L through metabolic engineering.
Aroma compound with floral odor, produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae CA strain. Production increased 9.05-fold to 13.87 mg/L through metabolic engineering.
α,ω-dicarboxylic acid considered as a potential product from further oxidation of 8-hydroxyoctanoic acid, though not significantly produced under the tested conditions.

A biological precursor for adipic acid, produced via engineered shikimate pathway branches in Saccharomyces cerevisiae and Escherichia coli. Engineered pathways include DHS-pathway, ANTH-pathway, DHBA-pathway, SA-pathway, and pHBA-pathway.

Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae for overproduction of TAGs, reaching up to 254 mg∙gCDW−1 (27.4% of the maximum theoretical yield) through metabolic engineering strategies including overexpression of ACC1**, PAH1, and DGA1, and deletion of TAG lipase genes (TGL3, TGL4, TGL5) and sterol acyltransferase gene (ARE1).
Engineered industrial Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains (e.g., HX57D, Z4X-21-18, H18s28) with enhanced xylose fermentation capacity and inhibitor tolerance for lignocellulosic ethanol production.

A novel bioactive ganoderic acid produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through heterologous expression of CYP5150L8 and CYP5139G1 enzymes. It is synthesized from lanosterol via successive oxidation and hydroxylation steps.
Intermediate in DHLDOA biosynthesis, produced by oxidation of lanosterol catalyzed by CYP5150L8.

A bioactive ginsenoside with diverse bioactivities including hepatoprotection, anti-diabetes, and anti-cancer activities. It is a major functional component absorbed into systemic circulation after oral administration of ginseng.
CA is an intermediate in the production of pHCA, formed from L-phenylalanine via PAL activity. It can be further hydroxylated to produce pHCA.
Triterpenoid backbone of celastrol, produced by the friedelin synthase KdFRS in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
Intermediate in the biosynthesis of polpunonic acid, produced by the oxidation of friedelin by CYP712K2 and CYP712K3.
Intermediate in the biosynthesis of polpunonic acid, produced by further oxidation of 29-hydroxy-friedelin.
Synthesized via thiolysis using Eat1 in vitro, with ethyl acetate and ethanethiol as substrates. Represents a novel thioester aroma compound.
Structural isomer of melatonin with antioxidant activity; produced in YNBT400 and whole-cell biotransformation trials.
Structural isomer of melatonin with antioxidant activity; detected alongside MI1 in optimized media.

Recombinant endoglucanase enzyme produced by engineered E. coli. Hydrolyzes cellulose in agricultural waste (e.g., red rice residues) into simple sugars for bioethanol production. Optimal activity at pH 6.0 and 60°C, stable across pH 4.0–8.0 and temperatures up to 60°C. Shows high specificity for β-(1→4) linkages in substrates like carboxymethylcellulose and β-d-glucan.
Produced in engineered Yarrowia lipolytica via overexpression of tHMG1, ERG20, POT1, and promoter engineering.

A commercially valuable triterpenoid precursor produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through metabolic engineering and transcriptional regulation, achieving a titer of 138.80 mg/L with a yield of 16.30 mg/g dry cell weight.

Produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing glutamate decarboxylases from Streptomyces sp. MJ654-NF4 (SsGAD) and Streptomyces chromofuscus ATCC 49982 (ScGAD) through whole-cell bioconversion of L-glutamate. Achieved accumulated production of 62.6 g/L after 10 batch cycles with 60.8% overall molar conversion rate.

A value-added chemical produced from engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via a de novo pathway involving heterologous expression of INO1, INM1, MIOX, and udh genes. Maximum titers reached 1.6 g/L from glucose supplemented with myo-inositol.
Main byproduct acting as redox sink for NAD+ regeneration during 2,3-butanediol fermentation, reaching 85.1 g/L in fed-batch cultivation
Minor byproduct accumulating to less than 2.5 g/L during 2,3-butanediol fermentation, particularly when galactose was solely consumed after glucose depletion

Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain JHY5730 produces D-lactic acid under acidic fermentation conditions (pH 3.5) with high titer (82.6 g/L), yield (0.83 g/g glucose), and productivity (1.50 g/(L·h)) in fed-batch fermentation.
Microbial oil feedstock for biodiesel production, accumulated in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae using glycerol as substrate via overexpression of GUT1, DGA1, and LRO1 genes
Microbial precursor for L-ephedrine synthesis produced by immobilized Saccharomyces cerevisiae via biotransformation of benzaldehyde using pyruvate decarboxylase
Most important ester in young wines, produced by Saccharomyces cerevisiae during fermentation, influencing wine aroma.
Volatile acid produced by Saccharomyces cerevisiae during fermentation, influencing wine acidity and aroma.
Enriched syrup obtained as a residual sugar after selective fermentation of glucose from sucrose, using hexokinase-deficient S. cerevisiae mutants unable to metabolize fructose.

A monoterpene alcohol produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through metabolic engineering of the mevalonate pathway, GPP synthesis regulation, and fusion protein strategies, achieving a titer of 293 mg/L in fed-batch cultivation.

Biodiesel produced from palm oil isolated from instant noodle waste via enzymatic transesterification using immobilized lipase Novozym 435, with a maximum conversion yield of 95.4% under optimized conditions.

SAM is synthesized by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through overexpression of the methionine adenosyltransferase gene SAM2. The recombinant strain R1-ZJU001 achieved 8.81 g/L SAM in a 15-liter fermentor, a 27.1% increase over the parent strain.

Bioethanol was produced from cotton gin waste through maleic acid pretreatment, enzymatic hydrolysis, and fermentation using sequential use of Saccharomyces cerevisiae and Pichia stipitis yeast strains. The process achieved an ethanol concentration of 18.74 g/l with a productivity of 2.25 g/l/h and a yield of 0.48 g/g.
Produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through optimization of the l-tyrosine metabolic pathway, including overexpression of tyrosine ammonia-lyase (TAL) from Rhodobacter capsulatus.
Cellulase enzyme was produced via solid-state fermentation using Trichoderma harzianum PBLA and water hyacinth as substrate, achieving a maximum activity of 2 U/g of dry matter.

Linalool is a monoterpene alcohol with a pleasant floral scent, widely used in the food, pharmaceutical, and cosmetics industries. It was produced in Saccharomyces cerevisiae by engineering the mevalonate pathway and optimizing linalool synthase activity.
A co-product of ethanol production, used as animal feed. The yield is approximately 6.4 lb per gallon of ethanol.
Furfural removed during detoxification process, with up to 74% removal efficiency.
Biodiesel produced from spent coffee grounds oil via ultrasound-assisted esterification with a yield of 97%. The oil was extracted using ultrasound-assisted solvent extraction with hexane.
A Schiff base formed by the condensation of aldehyde and cysteine during the detoxification process, identified as a major detoxification product.
Intermediate ketose produced from xylose isomerisation, which can be metabolised by Saccharomyces cerevisiae through the pentose phosphate pathway.

fields.grna_map: ERG7, CIT2, MLS1, ADH1, ADH4, ADH5, ADH6
fields.description: A pentacyclic triterpenoid compound produced in Saccharomyces cerevisiae by down-regulating competitive pathways using CRISPRi and optimizing fermentation conditions.
An FPP-derived byproduct accumulated during sesquiterpene production in engineered yeast strains, resulting from dephosphorylation of FPP.
Triterpenoid precursor to protopanaxadiol, co-produced during the engineered biosynthesis pathway in yeast, reaching 1548 mg/L in two-phase extractive fermentation.

Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain produces 1-butanol through an endogenous threonine-dependent pathway. Metabolic engineering strategies included ADH1 deletion, pathway enzyme overexpression, and elimination of competing pathways, achieving a titer of 242.8 mg/L from glucose.
Dimethylxanthine intermediate produced through heterologous N-methyltransferase activity in engineered yeast strains.
Dimethylxanthine produced as alternative product through promiscuous enzyme activity in engineered pathways.
Lathyrane diterpenoid backbone produced by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae from sugar via casbene synthase (CBS) with protein tagging strategies achieving ~160 mg/L titer
Orange betalain pigment produced by feeding anthranilic acid to betalamic acid-producing yeast strains.
Violet betaxanthin pigment produced by feeding 6-aminoindole to betalamic acid-producing yeast strains.
Red-violet single condensate and blue-violet double condensate pigments produced by feeding o-dianisidine to betalamic acid-producing yeast strains.

Valuable specialty chemical used as precursor for flavors, cosmetics, and pharmaceuticals including semisynthetic penicillins, cephalosporins, anti-obesity agents, and anti-HIV/anti-leukemic drugs. Produced via engineered hydroxymandelate synthase converting phenylpyruvate to mandelic acid.
A by-product of the tyrosine branch of the Ehrlich pathway, reduced through TYR1 promoter engineering to minimize competition with 2-phenylethanol production.

11α-hydroxylation by-product produced alongside hydrocortisone in engineered yeast system. Present in 1:4 molar ratio with hydrocortisone in the engineered system compared to 1:1 ratio in native A. orchidis.
Mono-N-methylated pathway intermediate detected in psilocybin-producing strains.
Non-N-methylated pathway intermediate detected in psilocybin-producing strains.
Dephosphorylated degradation product of baeocystin detected in engineered strains.
Pathway intermediate accumulating at 120.3 ± 11.1 mg/L in initial strains before pathway optimization.

High-value plant-derived triterpene with strong antiproliferative activity, produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via protein engineering of α-amyrin synthase (MdOSC1) and metabolic engineering of the MVA pathway

Acyclic monoterpene alcohol produced through engineered metabolic pathway in Saccharomyces cerevisiae using a 'push-pull-restrain' strategy. Highest titer achieved was 8.30 g/L in fed-batch fermentation.
Core triterpene aglycone precursor for PPT-type saponins, produced by engineered yeast chassis strain through optimization of PgCYP716A53v2 cytochrome P450
Oligosaccharide produced during starch hydrolysis by Rhynchosia root extract, representing 17% of total sugars but not utilized by the microorganisms during fermentation.
Yeast transformants produced resveratrol L-glucoside (piceid), which was converted to free resveratrol by L-glucosidase treatment.

An intermediate ester produced from crotonyl-CoA and ethanol, accumulated when butyryl-CoA dehydrogenase (Bcd) was rate-limiting. Production decreased when Bcd was replaced with trans-enoyl-CoA reductase (Ter).

Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain α(L)-tCP-tAeΔpor2 produced 420.48 ± 6.03 mg/L ethyl lactate through introduction of propionyl coenzyme A transferase (Pct) and alcohol acyltransferase (AAT) genes, increased gene copy numbers, and knockout of porin gene por2 to impede mitochondrial pyruvate transport.
A high-value flavor compound with caramel-like aroma, produced in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae through metabolic engineering and enzyme engineering of enone oxidoreductase.
Intermediate in CBD biosynthesis pathway, converted from cannabigerolic acid by cannabidiol acid synthase (CBDAS) in engineered yeast.
Key intermediate in cannabinoid biosynthesis pathway, transported from cytoplasm to vacuole by BPT1 transporter to enhance CBD production efficiency.
Precursor compound in CBD biosynthesis pathway formed from malonyl coenzyme A and hexanoyl coenzyme A, with production optimized through metabolic engineering.

Polyphenolic flavonoid with health benefits including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, anticancer, and anti-atherogenic activities. Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae achieved 86 mg/L production via metabolic engineering strategies.
Compound accumulated during fermentation, particularly from whole wheat flour, reaching up to 0.63 g/L.

C8:0 fatty alcohol produced via engineered pathway in Saccharomyces cerevisiae using carboxylic acid reductase (CAR) from Mycobacterium marinum and phosphopantetheinyl transferase (SFP) from Bacillus subtilis to convert accumulated free fatty acids.
C10:0 fatty alcohol synthesized by engineered S. cerevisiae strain through CAR/SFP conversion of free fatty acids accumulated via deletion of FAA1 and FAA4 genes.
C12:0 fatty alcohol produced in engineered yeast via metabolic pathway enhancing free fatty acid supply and CAR/SFP enzymatic conversion.
C14:0 fatty alcohol generated from myristic acid by engineered S. cerevisiae with enhanced FFA accumulation and CAR/SFP expression.
C16:1 unsaturated fatty alcohol, the most abundant product (up to 14.1 mg/L) from palmitoleic acid via engineered yeast pathway with CAR/SFP.
C18:0 fatty alcohol synthesized from stearic acid by engineered S. cerevisiae strain with enhanced FFA accumulation and CAR/SFP conversion.
C18:1 unsaturated fatty alcohol produced from oleic acid via CAR/SFP pathway in metabolically engineered yeast.
Reporter protein expressed under control of the CuIGR4 system, showing 72-fold induction with 20 μM copper addition and 2.7-fold higher expression compared to direct CUP1 promoter control.
A heterologous product synthesized in Y. lipolytica via assembly of the vioABE pathway, used to test feasibility of the Yl-TU assembly toolkit.
Sweeteners including stevioside, rebaudioside A, Reb D, and Reb M, produced by glucosylating steviol with UDP-glucosyltransferases
Diterpene intermediate produced from glucose via mevalonate pathway, GGPPS, CPPS, and KS enzymes
Oxidized intermediate from kaurene via KO activity, accumulates when KAH activity is limiting

Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae produced approximately 100 mg/L of (S)-phenylethanediol after 96 h cultivation via artificial enzyme cascades from L-phenylalanine. The pathway involves PAL2, FDC1, SMO, and SpEH enzymes with enantiomeric purity above 97%.

Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae produced approximately 110 mg/L of (R)-phenylethanediol after 96 h cultivation via artificial enzyme cascades from L-phenylalanine. The pathway involves PAL2, FDC1, SMO, and StEH enzymes with enantiomeric purity above 99%.

A six-carbon dicarboxylic acid with two conjugated double bonds, used as a platform chemical for nylon, polyurethanes, terephthalic acid, and functional resins/plastics. Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain produced 20.8 g/L in fed-batch fermentation.
Organic acid monitored during fermentation; no remarkable differences due to SO2 treatment.
Higher alcohol derived from isoleucine; affected by SO2 treatment.
Higher alcohol derived from leucine; affected by SO2 treatment.

Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae biomass containing high concentrations of organically bound selenium, primarily as selenomethionine, produced via continuous fermentation with optimized selenium/sulfur ratios
Novel compound (DMG) produced as intermediate during CK biosynthesis through glucosylation of dammarenediol II by UGTPg1
Intermediate in nootkatone biosynthesis, produced at 30 mg/L by engineered E. coli and S. cerevisiae co-culture
Production of alkanes from FFAs via a pathway involving carboxylic acid reductase (CAR) and aldehyde deformylating oxygenase (ADO), achieving the highest reported titre in S. cerevisiae.
Protoberberine alkaloid intermediate produced early in the noscapine pathway via PsTNMT activity in engineered yeast.
Protoberberine intermediate synthesized by CYP82Y1 in yeast, optimized for high titers through promoter and temperature engineering.
Pathway intermediate generated via CYP82X2 activity, structurally validated through NMR in yeast production.
Acetylated protoberberine intermediate formed by PsAT1, serving as a substrate for downstream secoberberine conversion.
Secoberberine alkaloid synthesized by CYP82X1, structurally unique and validated via NMR in yeast.
Cyclized intermediate produced spontaneously or via PsCXE1 activity, convertible to narcotoline by PsSDR1.
Monoterpene hydrocarbon synthesized in engineered yeast. Industrial applications include flavors, antibacterials, insecticides, and green plastics.
Monoterpene produced via orthogonal pathway in yeast. Used in flavors, antibacterial agents, insecticides, and high-density biofuels.
Monoterpene scaffold generated by engineered yeast. Employed in flavorings, antimicrobial products, and renewable materials.
Bicyclic monoterpene synthesized using engineered enzymes. Applications include fragrances, flavors, insecticides, and high-density renewable fuels.
Produced at 2.3 g/L in fed-batch cultures via systematic engineering of Saccharomyces cerevisiae metabolism, serving as a central metabolite for polyamine analogues.
Unnatural halogenated polyamine-hydroxycinnamic acid conjugates (e.g., N1-3-fluorocoumaroyl spermidine) synthesized via enzyme promiscuity and feeding fluorinated precursors.

Bioactive dammarenediol-II glucoside produced de novo in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae via metabolic engineering and CRISPR/Cas9 optimization. Exhibits anti-colon cancer activity. Achieved titer of 2.4 g/L in fed-batch fermentation.

Bioactive dammarenediol-II glucoside synthesized in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae using codon-optimized genes and metabolic pathway refactoring. Demonstrates enhanced anti-colon cancer properties. Reached 5.6 g/L titer in bioreactor fermentation.

Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing cytochrome P450 monooxygenase (CYP7B1) converts dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) into 7α-OH-DHEA using a bioelectrocatalysis system (BES) with neutral red-mediated extracellular electron transfer to regenerate NAD(P)H cofactors.

Synthesized from β-nootkatol oxidation by alcohol dehydrogenase (ADH1) in engineered S. cerevisiae. Optimized strains produced 53.7 mg/L ethyl acetate. Used in flavors, fragrances, and as an insect repellent.
Hydroxylated sapogenin produced by engineered yeast expressing β-amyrin synthase and CYP716Y1, which catalyzes C-16α hydroxylation of β-amyrin.
Hydroxylated sapogenin produced by engineered yeast expressing α-amyrin synthase and CYP716Y1, enabling C-16α hydroxylation of α-amyrin.
Combinatorial sapogenin with hydroxylation at C-16 and oxidation to aldehyde at C-28, synthesized in yeast co-expressing CYP716Y1 and CYP716A12 with β-amyrin synthase.
Monoglucosylated saponin produced in yeast expressing β-amyrin synthase, CYP716A12 (for C-28 oxidation), and UGT73C11 (for C-3 glucosylation).
Monoglucosylated saponin synthesized in yeast via combinatorial expression of β-amyrin synthase, CYP716Y1 (C-16 hydroxylation), CYP716A12 (C-28 oxidation), and UGT73C11 (C-3 glucosylation).
Fructooligosaccharide produced via enzymatic transfructosylation using invertases from Saccharomyces cerevisiae CAT-1 and Rhodotorula mucilaginosa at high sucrose concentrations.

Enhanced production via co-expression of aminotransferase gene ARO8 and decarboxylase gene ARO10 in engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain S810, achieving 3.24 g/L yield in fed-batch fermentation.

Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains produced taxadiene, a key intermediate in paclitaxel (anti-cancer drug) biosynthesis, achieving a 25-fold improvement via solubility-enhanced taxadiene synthase (TXS-MBP fusion).
Includes 2-phenylethanol, 2-methyl-1-butanol, 3-methyl-1-butanol, 1-propanol, and 2-methyl-1-propanol; concentrations varied significantly (P<0.001) among strains, impacting wine flavor.
Vinylphenol produced from ferulic acid via engineered yeast metabolism, involved in off-flavor development during wine fermentation.
Antimicrobial peptide produced by engineered yeast via introduced Lactococcus lactis genes (NisA, B, C, P) to inhibit lactic acid bacteria contamination during ethanol fermentation.
Group of alcohols significantly more abundant in SmF (approximately 5× higher than SSF), influenced by specific strains like genotypes II, IV, and VIII.

Undesirable by-product of alcoholic fermentation in wine production, influenced by nutrient interactions (thiamin, pyridoxine, and YAN). Higher concentrations occur under vitamin-deficient conditions.
Side product generated from dihydrocinnamoyl-CoA through engineered flavonoid pathway in yeast.
Major higher alcohol elevated in GAT1- and CAN1-deleted strains; reduced in BAT2-deleted strains. Critical for Baijiu aroma.
Sesquiterpene hydrocarbon precursor produced via metabolic engineering of Saccharomyces cerevisiae to redirect carbon flux through the mevalonate pathway.
Engineered CBH1 enzyme secreted by Saccharomyces cerevisiae for efficient hydrolysis of crystalline cellulose.
High-yield CBH2 enzyme produced in yeast, enabling synergistic cellulose degradation in biofuel production.
Engineered bimodular cellulase with enhanced cellulose-binding capacity for improved hydrolysis efficiency.
Co-secreted cellulase combination enabling synergistic hydrolysis of cellulose for consolidated bioprocessing.
Biodegradable polyester synthesized via engineered PHB pathway in Saccharomyces cerevisiae for industrial bioplastic production.
C15 alkane produced via decarbonylation of fatty aldehydes by engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing cyanobacterial aldehyde deformylating oxygenases (ADOs).
C15:1 alkene synthesized by engineered yeast expressing ADOs from Synechococcus elongatus or Crocosphaera watsonii.
C17 alkane generated through fatty aldehyde conversion by cyanobacterial ADOs in metabolically engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
C11 alkane detected in yeast strains co-expressing ADOs from Crocosphaera watsonii and Synechococcus elongatus under dynamic GAL promoters.

Precursor to levopimaric acid, produced at 49.21 mg/L via N-terminal truncation and mutagenesis (M593I/Y700F) of levopimaradiene synthase (t79LPSMM) in engineered S. cerevisiae chassis with enhanced GGPP flux.

Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae produced valencene at 539.3 mg/L via metabolic engineering, including CRISPR/Cas9-mediated downregulation of squalene synthesis (erg9), knockout of inhibiting factors (rox1, bts1, ypl062w, yjl064w), overexpression of FPP pathway genes (erg10, erg13, tHMG1, erg12, erg8, erg19, idi1, erg20), and optimization of valencene synthase expression cassettes. This represents a 160-fold improvement over the initial strain.

Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain WLN-3 overexpressing PGM2, UGP1, and UGT1 genes produces ginsenoside CK, reaching 263.94 mg/L in flasks and 1.70 g/L in a 5-L bioreactor with glycerol/ethanol co-feeding.

Retrieved as a by-product during ionic liquid/acid/metal ion pretreatment. Extracted into acetone-water anti-solvent and recovered after evaporation, yielding ~21 wt% of original biomass. Potential applications include carbon fiber or vanillin production.
Produced via microbial coculture engineering for applications in nutraceuticals and pharmaceuticals.
